column2: The mushrooms from the Fairy Ring are pretty good, so you hear.  However, when they're mixed with Kobold toadstools...that's a horse of a different color.

column1: You eat some of the Kobold's fungus.  Within seconds your insides feel like they're on fire.  Guess you're not a Kobold, huh?
column2: 你吃了一些地精的菌類。幾秒內你的內臟就像著火一樣。看來你不是地精，是吧?

column1: You think that you'd better take it easy for a while until you recover.
column2: 你認為你最好休息一陣子，直到康復為止。

column1: You ingest a few of the mushrooms from the Faery Ring.  They taste delicious.
column2: You ingest a few of the mushrooms from the Faery Ring.  They taste delicious.

column1: Wow!  Look at all the nice paisley horses!
column2: 哇!看看所有漂亮的西洋花紋馬!

column1: Not to mention the beautiful neon sky.
column2: 更別說那美麗的霓虹天空。

column1: That's funny.  I could have sworn I warned you about eating too many Magic Mushrooms.  Your mind permanently warped, you die a garishly polka-dotted death.
column2: 真搞笑。我發誓我警告過你別吃太多魔法蘑菇。你的心智永久變形,以花俏的圓點花紋死亡。

column1: Hmm, that was interesting.  But it would probably not be a good idea to repeat the experience.
column2: 嗯，那很有趣。但重複那種經驗可能不是個好主意。

column1: You eat the Magic Acorn.  It tastes terrible.  Better stick to real food next time.
column2: 你吃了魔法橡實。味道很糟。下次還是堅持吃真正的食物。

column1: There's no need.  You're not hungry.
column2: 沒必要。你不餓。

column1: You eat some of the apples.  They actually taste quite good.
column2: 你吃了一些蘋果。它們實際上味道相當不錯。

column1: You don't have any fruit.
column2: You don't have any fruit.

column2: Ugh.  You don't want to eat *that*.

column1: While you were asleep, something decided to make a meal of you.  You're not sure what it was, but you don't really care at this point.  You shouldn't go to sleep where the creatures of the night can get you.
column2: 你睡著時,什麼東西決定拿你當大餐。你不確定是什麼,但現在你真的不在乎。你不應該在夜間生物能抓到你的地方睡覺。

column1: You're too impatient to rest right now.
column2: 你現在太焦躁,靜不下來休息。

column1: After %d minutes rest, you feel better.
column2: 休息了%d分鐘後，你感覺好多了。

column1: Congratulations!\nYou have discovered the first bug in this game.
column2: Congratulations!\nYou have discovered the first bug in this game.

column1: In a hole, hidden beneath the ancient stone, someone has placed a scroll.
column2: 在這塊古老石頭下方的洞裡，有人放了一卷捲軸。

column1: The scroll is no longer here.
column2: The scroll is no longer here.

column1: You see no scroll here.
column2: You see no scroll here.

column1: The scroll vanishes even as you read the magical runes upon it.   You now have the knowledge to cast a "Calm" spell.
column2: 當你讀著捲軸上的魔法符文時，捲軸化為虛無。你現在已經掌握了施放「鎮定術」的知識。

column1: Your hunger has been totally satisfied.  You don't need another piece of fruit.
column2: 你已經完全飽了。不需要再吃水果了。

column1: The stone has the words "Guruka's Peace" carved upon the top.  Funny.  You thought it said something else the last time you looked at it.
column2: The stone has the words "Guruka's Peace" carved upon the top.  Funny.  You thought it said something else the last time you looked at it.

column1: The stone has the words "Erana's Peace" carved upon the top.  Maybe you need glasses.
column2: The stone has the words "Erana's Peace" carved upon the top.  Maybe you need glasses.

column1: The stone has the words "Erana's Peace" carved upon the top.  There are some runes carved along the side.
column2: 石頭的頂端刻著「伊瑞娜的安寧之地」字樣。側面還刻著一些符文。

column1: It reads:  "If thy Will is Magic, so shall I share.  Open this Stone and claim what is there."
column2: 內容寫著：「若汝意志乃魔法者，吾願與爾分享。開啟此石，領取所有寶藏。」

column1: No amount of physical effort will be able to move this stone.
column2: 再怎麼費力氣也動不了這塊石頭。

column1: The meadow lies covered with a blanket of flowers, unusual for this early in the spring.  It is warm, even though surrounded by the late snows of winter.
column2: 春天才剛開始，草地上卻已經覆滿了一片花海——這很不尋常。儘管被冬天的殘雪包圍，這裡卻溫暖如春。

column1: The air has the fresh, clean scent of the mountains, accompanied by numerous perfume-like fragrances.
column2: 空氣中飄散著山區的清新氣息，混合著各式各樣香氛般的花香。

column1: A large, carved stone lies flat on the ground.  You feel as though someone gentle was watching over you.  You feel that you are safe here.
column2: 一塊巨大的石雕平放在地上。你感到彷彿有某個溫柔的人在守護著你。你感到自己在這裡很安全。

column1: All kinds of colorful and fragrant flowers and grasses cover the meadow.
column2: 各式各樣的繽紛花朵和香草覆蓋著整個草地。

column1: The sky is clear.
column2: 天空晴朗無雲。

column1: The stars shine.
column2: 繁星閃爍。

column1: The sun feels warm.
column2: 陽光暖洋洋的。

column1: The small tree is most amazing.  It bears blossoms and fruit at the same time, and the fruit on its boughs seems to shyly appear and disappear, shimmering.
column2: 這棵小樹最是不可思議。它同時開著花和結著果，枝頭上的果實似乎害羞地時隱時現、閃爍著。

column1: The tree's blossoms are soft, green and fragrant.
column2: 樹上的花朵柔軟、青翠又芬芳。

column1: The fruit glows and shimmers in the moonlight.  It produces a fragrance like a sun-ripened strawberry.
column2: 果實在月光下閃閃發亮、搖搖欲墜。散發出像熟透草莓的香氣。

column1: The fruit sparkles in the sunlight, yet it appears soft and fuzzy.
column2: 果實在陽光下閃閃發光，但看起來軟軟的、毛毛的。

column1: To the north, the high, snowy peaks of the Dragon Smoke mountains are clearly visible.
column2: 北方遠處，龍煙山脈高聳的雪峰清晰可見。

column1: The meadow is an oasis at the edge of the snowline.  The rocky hillside looks very steep and slippery.
column2: 這個草地是雪線邊緣的綠洲。岩石山坡看起來陡峭又滑溜。

column1: You are at the snowline.  All around you, except for the forest, the rocks and mountains glisten with slippery snow.
column2: 你已經來到雪線。除了森林以外的四周，岩石和山脈都被滑溜的積雪所覆蓋。

column2: You don't think you can climb any further up the cliff.

column1: You've never seen anything quite like them. They're pretty, in a grotesque way.
column2: 你從來沒見過這樣的東西。它們有種怪異的美感。

column2: At least, they're pretty when they're not dead.

column1: That's one mean Ogress!
column2: 那真是隻狠辣的女食人妖！

column1: There's no time for idle chatter.
column2: 沒時間閒聊。

column1: There is a bat, all folded up within his wings, hanging on the wall.
column2: 有隻蝙蝠用翅膀把自己包起來，掛在牆上。

column1: Is that a SMILE on that spider's face?
column2: 那隻蜘蛛的臉上有微笑嗎？

column2: You HATE houses with cobwebs in the corners, especially when there's an immense spider squatting cheerily in the middle of the cobweb.

column2: The glass seems to distort the view of the outside world.

column2: The kitchen knives look old but sharp.

column2: You might as well be talking to yourself.

column2: Your magic is useless here.

column2:

column1: Well Dearies!  What shall we have for supper today?

column1: Hero sandwiches?\nI had something more formal in mind.
column2: 「英雄三明治？我想要點更正式的。」

column1: Ah...that's it!\nFrog Legs Fricassee.
column2: 「啊…那就是了！炸青蛙腿。」

column2: Now how does that spell go.....?

column1:
Hear me, oh Powers
Of Klatha and Mana!
Turn now my guest

column2: Those brutes!  I'm always having to heal someone they've beaten up and robbed.  I hate thieves and brigands.

column1: Would you believe the brigands almost killed him a while back?  Fortunately their leader made them bring Heinrich here where I could heal him.
column2: 「你能相信山賊曾幾乎殺死他嗎？幸好他們的首領命令他們把海因里希帶到我這裡，我才能救他。」

column1: Almost due north of here is the meadow called Erana's Peace.  It is a very magical area and it is a place of safety.  It is beautiful all year, for the flowers are always in bloom.
column2: Almost due north of here is the meadow called Erana's Peace.  It is a very magical area and it is a place of safety.  It is beautiful all year, for the flowers are always in bloom.

column1: I have the skill to use my magic to create potions.  I am a bit proud about it.  Not everyone can do that, you know.
column2: I have the skill to use my magic to create potions.  I am a bit proud about it.  Not everyone can do that, you know.

column1: Mandrake root is used in a variety of spells, mostly for evil purposes.  Mandrake must be pulled from a deadman's grave at midnight.  The root is particularly powerful.
column2: Mandrake root is used in a variety of spells, mostly for evil purposes.  Mandrake must be pulled from a deadman's grave at midnight.  The root is particularly powerful.

column1: I'm sorry, I just don't know much about that.  Perhaps you should ask someone else.
column2: I'm sorry, I just don't know much about that.  Perhaps you should ask someone else.

column1: Oh, thank you for finding my ring.  How I've missed this.  Here are six golds and two Healing Potions for your reward!
column2: 「哦，謝謝你找到我的戒指。我多麼想念它。這是六枚金幣和兩瓶治療藥水作為你的獎勵！」

column1: Thank you, but I have all the flowers I need.
column2: Thank you, but I have all the flowers I need.

column1: Thank you.  I often use flowers from Erana's Peace in potion making.  Here are your silvers.\nYou put them away.
column2: Thank you.  I often use flowers from Erana's Peace in potion making.  Here are your silvers.\nYou put them away.

column1: Thank you, but I have enough mushrooms.
column2: Thank you, but I have enough mushrooms.

column1: You have ruined the mushrooms with toadstools.  I can not buy your mushrooms.
column2: You have ruined the mushrooms with toadstools.  I can not buy your mushrooms.

column1: These are very nice.  I'll dry them and grind them into a powder.  Let me think...oh yes! I said these are worth a gold.  Here you are.\nYou put it away.
column2: These are very nice.  I'll dry them and grind them into a powder.  Let me think...oh yes! I said these are worth a gold.  Here you are.\nYou put it away.

column1: Do you mean to tell me that you actually managed to kill a Cheetaur?  You're quite a hero, aren't you?  Here is your money.\nYou might think about purchasing a Healing Potion in case you have to fight such monsters again.
column2: Do you mean to tell me that you actually managed to kill a Cheetaur?  You're quite a hero, aren't you?  Here is your money.\nYou might think about purchasing a Healing Potion in case you have to fight such monsters again.

column1: Don't tell me you actually killed a Troll?  Why, you are really amazing.  I haven't had any Troll's Beard for a long time.  Here are two Healing Potions for the beard.\nYou put them away.
column2: Don't tell me you actually killed a Troll?  Why, you are really amazing.  I haven't had any Troll's Beard for a long time.  Here are two Healing Potions for the beard.\nYou put them away.

column1: So, you say this is one of the ingredients for the Dispel Potion.  Well, I had better get started making it.
column2: 「所以，你說這是解除魔法藥水的一種材料。好吧，我最好開始製作它。」

column1: Thank you.  I'm sure that I'll find a good use for Fairy Dust.
column2: 「謝謝。我相信我會找到妖精粉的好用途。」

column1: I don't need any more Flying Water.  Thank you anyway.
column2: 「我不需要更多飛水了。不過還是謝謝。」

column1: Thank you for the offer, but I really don't need any water.
column2: 「謝謝你的提議，但我真的不需要任何水。」

column1: The Healer makes some sort of arcane gesture over the flask of water, then looks surprised.
column2: 治療師對著水瓶做出某種奧祕的手勢，然後看起來很驚訝。

column1: I'm sorry, but this water does not seem to have any magical potential at all.  This can't be the `Flying Water' you described.
column2: 「抱歉，但這水似乎完全沒有任何魔法潛力。這不可能是你描述的『飛水』。」

column1: The Healer makes some sort of arcane gesture over each flask of water, then smiles.
column2: The Healer makes some sort of arcane gesture over each flask of water, then smiles.

column1: The Healer makes some sort of arcane gesture over the flask of water, then smiles.
column2: 治療師對著水瓶做出某種奧祕的手勢，然後微笑。

column1: Flying Water. how clever.
column2: Flying Water. how clever.

column1: Those Meeps sound so interesting.  I'd like to meet them sometime.  I'll get to work on that potion of yours.
column2: 「那些咪普聽起來很有趣。我想有機會見見它們。我來開始製作你的藥水吧。」

column1: Thank you.  I'll save this for a later use.
column2: 「謝謝。我會存起來以後用。」

column1: So you helped the Dryad, that's nice.  She does keep the forest around here healthy.  So that's how to make a Dispel Potion, is it?  Thanks for letting me know.
column2: So you helped the Dryad, that's nice.  She does keep the forest around here healthy.  So that's how to make a Dispel Potion, is it?  Thanks for letting me know.

column1: Thank you, I always need flasks.
column2: 「謝謝，我總是需要藥瓶。」

column1: I don't have any use for that.
column2: I don't have any use for that.

column1: You'll have to be more specific.
column2: You'll have to be more specific.

column1: I need all my flasks for potions.  You'll have to get one somewhere else.
column2: I need all my flasks for potions.  You'll have to get one somewhere else.

column1: I don't have any.
column2: I don't have any.

column1: You're getting greedy.  She'll notice if you take any more.
column2: 你貪心了。你再拿她就會發現的。

column1: You carefully grab a couple of Healing Potions and conceal them under your cape.
column2: 你小心翼翼地抓起幾瓶治療藥水，藏在你的披風下。

column1: If you're going to steal, at least wait until the Healer is not looking.
column2: 你要偷的話，至少等治療師不看的時候。

column1: You don't really think that is the correct way to actually win this game, do you?
column2: You don't really think that is the correct way to actually win this game, do you?

column1: Please, show some respect.
column2: Please, show some respect.

column1: There is an aura of magic throughout this small cabin.
column2: 整間小屋籠罩著魔法氣息。

column1: Small on a prehistoric scale, but still a pterosaur.
column2: 按史前標準來說很小，但仍然是翼龍。

column1: Cheesecloth.
column2: Cheesecloth.

column1: Ladder up to the loft where the Healer has her personal quarters.  No, you can't go up the ladder.
column2: Ladder up to the loft where the Healer has her personal quarters.  No, you can't go up the ladder.

column1: The makings of various potions.
column2: The makings of various potions.

column1: The pterosaur's litter box.
column2: 翼龍的貓砂盆。

column1: Iron holder fit for a lit torch.
column2: Iron holder fit for a lit torch.

column1: The view from the window has been obscured by soot from the fire.
column2: 窗戶外的景色被爐火的煙灰遮住了。

column1: This and that.
column2: This and that.

column1: Ingredients.
column2: Ingredients.

column1: Petrified icicles and greens.
column2: Petrified icicles and greens.

column1: The fragrance of the herbs mingle with some other rather pungent odors as you step into the Healer's house.
column2: The fragrance of the herbs mingle with some other rather pungent odors as you step into the Healer's house.

column1: Well, what can I do for you this time?
column2: 「嗯，這次我能為你做什麼？」

column1: Lovely day, isn't it?  My, you look very healthy for an adventurer.  You must be new.
column2: 「美好的一天，不是嗎？哇，你作為一個冒險家看起來很健康。你一定是新手。」

column1: What can I do for you?  I can sell you potions if you like.  I also buy spell components if you are interested in gathering some for me.
column2: What can I do for you?  I can sell you potions if you like.  I also buy spell components if you are interested in gathering some for me.

column1: Don't mind me, I always have so much to do around here.
column2: Don't mind me, I always have so much to do around here.

column1: You say to the Healer "I have been to visit the Dryad of the Woods.  She gave me a Magic Acorn and told me the formula for a potion to dispel enchantments!"
column2: You say to the Healer "I have been to visit the Dryad of the Woods.  She gave me a Magic Acorn and told me the formula for a potion to dispel enchantments!"

column1: Here is the Potion of Dispel!  Use it by splashing it on the victim of a magic spell.  Mind you, this won't work if the spell is caused by a magic item rather than a cast spell.
column2: Here is the Potion of Dispel!  Use it by splashing it on the victim of a magic spell.  Mind you, this won't work if the spell is caused by a magic item rather than a cast spell.

column1: Thank you so much.  Oh, I'm so happy to have my ring back!
column2: Thank you so much.  Oh, I'm so happy to have my ring back!

column1: You put the reward away and tell her she is welcome as you leave to avoid being kissed again.
column2: You put the reward away and tell her she is welcome as you leave to avoid being kissed again.

column1: The Dispel Potion will take a while to make.  Why don't you come back later?
column2: 「解除魔法藥水需要一些時間製作。你何不稍後回來？」

column1:
Brauggi I be, to boast of my boldness,
Strong as the storm that sends forth the snow.
Fiercer in fighting than foes in their fury,
Fear now this Frost Giant fighter and flee!
column2:
Brauggi I be, to boast of my boldness,
Strong as the storm that sends forth the snow.
Fiercer in fighting than foes in their fury,
Fear now this Frost Giant fighter and flee!

column1:
Find me some fruit for to mellow my mead horn,
Gift I will give of a gem that now glows --
Jewel from Jotunheim, flare of the frost flame,
Fetch to me fruit that will fill up my fists!
column2:
Find me some fruit for to mellow my mead horn,
Gift I will give of a gem that now glows --
Jewel from Jotunheim, flare of the frost flame,
Fetch to me fruit that will fill up my fists!

column1: (It looks like it will take a lot of fruit to fill up THOSE fists!)
column2: （要填滿那樣的拳頭看來得花不少水果啊！）

column1: From the blue color of his skin and his huge height, you can tell this is a Frost Giant.
column2: 從他皮膚的藍色和巨大的身軀，你能看出這是個暴風巨人。

column1: The Giant (who does not seem pleased at your casting a spell in his presence) radiates a magical aura.  The strongest magic centers on the glowing gem at his belt.
column2: 巨人（看起來對你在他面前施法不太高興）散發著魔法氣息。最強的魔法聚集在他腰上那顆會發光的寶石上。

column1: You can't offer what you don't have.
column2: You can't offer what you don't have.

column1: The Giant looks at you in disgust.  Apparently, you did not offer what he desires.
column2: 巨人厭惡地看著你。看來你沒給他想要的東西。

column1:
Near now is North, for the winter has wandered,
Bringing this brave one to barter so bold.
I journey from Jotunheim, home of the giants,
Source of all snowstorms, the sender of sleet.
column2:
Near now is North, for the winter has wandered,
Bringing this brave one to barter so bold.
I journey from Jotunheim, home of the giants,
Source of all snowstorms, the sender of sleet.

column1: Hollow my food house,\n        for my hunt has been hindered,\nBy having to hurry here from my home.
column2: 「我的食物倉空了啊，因為狩獵受阻，得匆匆從家趕來這裡。」

column1: Highpoint of harvest, the finest of flavor,\nApples or oranges, peaches or pears.
column2: 「豐收的高峰，最上乘的滋味，蘋果或柳橙，桃子或梨子。」

column1: Mead is the mother's milk, mighty yet mellow,\nThat brings joy to Giants and madness to men.
column2: Mead is the mother's milk, mighty yet mellow,\nThat brings joy to Giants and madness to men.

column1: Glow of the frost flame that fills up the night field,\nA jewel that is flawless, the finest of gems.
column2: 「充滿夜空的冰焰光芒，無瑕的寶石，最精美的珍珠。」

column1: Bored I become when the banter so blindly\nSpeaks of the subjects I care not about.
column2: Bored I become when the banter so blindly\nSpeaks of the subjects I care not about.

column1: Annoy me not over matters so minor.
column2: Annoy me not over matters so minor.

column1: Waste not my wits, for I weary of words;\nMy axe it is asking for action and more.
column2: Waste not my wits, for I weary of words;\nMy axe it is asking for action and more.

column1: The mountains rise quickly from this point and are covered with snow.
column2: The mountains rise quickly from this point and are covered with snow.

column1: The snow has melted in most of the forest, but here it is still heavy.
column2: The snow has melted in most of the forest, but here it is still heavy.

column1: The gem glows like an aurora in the night sky.
column2: 那顆寶石像夜空的極光一樣閃耀。

column1: You don't see a gem here.
column2: You don't see a gem here.

column1:
Far from the frost field, fares forth this fighter,
Hunger has hurled me hither from home.
My name it is known in the Northlands as Brauggi,
Barter with blade's clash, or bargain with me.
column2:
Far from the frost field, fares forth this fighter,
Hunger has hurled me hither from home.
My name it is known in the Northlands as Brauggi,
Barter with blade's clash, or bargain with me.

column1:
Fruit you have found, to fill all my food stores,
Thus filled the bargain, my gem you have bought.
Brauggi has bartered, and all has been answered;
The mead it may mellow, and now I head home.
column2:
Fruit you have found, to fill all my food stores,
Thus filled the bargain, my gem you have bought.
Brauggi has bartered, and all has been answered;
The mead it may mellow, and now I head home.

column1:
Fiercer than frost's bite, my fury is fearless,
My axe it is edged as is the ice.
This warrior is willing to wield now the war drum,
The battleaxe blazes and bellows for blood.
column2:
Fiercer than frost's bite, my fury is fearless,
My axe it is edged as is the ice.
This warrior is willing to wield now the war drum,
The battleaxe blazes and bellows for blood.

column1: Next time, pick on someone your own size.
column2: 下次找個你體格相當的對手。

column1: Courage you carry, but skill it is scanty;\nFace not this foe before fiercer you fight.
column2: 「你有膽量啊，但技巧還差得遠；別跟我這敵手交手，除非你變得更兇悍。」

column1: Your fruit definitely makes a generous pile in the Giant's cupped hands, but they're not full yet.
column2: 你的水果在巨人的雙手窩裡堆成不錯的一堆，但還沒滿。

column1: Oops!  you say.  "I guess I don't have any fruit for you.  Sorry!"
column2: Oops!  you say.  "I guess I don't have any fruit for you.  Sorry!"

column1: Your meagre selection of fruit makes a paltry pile at the bottom of the Giant's cupped hands.  You'll need to get quite a few more apples to fill THOSE hands!
column2: 你那可憐兮兮的水果在巨人雙手窩底堆成一小堆。你得多拿好幾個蘋果才能填滿那樣的手！

column1: As you start to throw...
column2: 當你開始投擲時…

column1: As you begin to cast your spell...
column2: 當你開始施法時…

column1: In matters of magic, I am your master; Seek not to slay me with sendings so small.
column2: In matters of magic, I am your master; Seek not to slay me with sendings so small.

column1: Running on the ice is apparently not a very good idea.
column2: 在冰上跑看起來不是什麼好主意。

column1: This large rock covers a Meep hole.
column2: This large rock covers a Meep hole.

column1: A furry blue Meep
column2: A furry blue Meep

column1: A furry purple Meep
column2: A furry purple Meep

column1: A furry green Meep
column2: A furry green Meep

column1: No way!  Those Meeps are too fast and the rocks too hard for you to want to try that again!
column2: 沒門！那些咪普太快、石頭太硬了，你不會想再試一次！

column1: Try going over to where the Meeps are if you really want to hack them into little furry bits.
column2: Try going over to where the Meeps are if you really want to hack them into little furry bits.

column1: You detect no other magic here.
column2: 你在這裡沒有偵測到其他魔法。

column1: The Open spell only works on locks and small closed things.
column2: 「開啟術」只對鎖和小型密閉物品有效。

column1: You don't have a rock
column2: You don't have a rock

column1: You can't get that from where you're standing.
column2: You can't get that from where you're standing.

column1: The Meeps would not appreciate it if you tried to take their fur -- the weather here is still very cold.
column2: The Meeps would not appreciate it if you tried to take their fur -- the weather here is still very cold.

column1: You don't see a Spell Scroll here.
column2: You don't see a Spell Scroll here.

column1: The baby Meep is just too quick for you.  Besides, they eat too much.
column2: 小咪普快得你根本追不上。而且它們食量太大了。

column1: You don't see any baby Meeps at the moment.
column2: You don't see any baby Meeps at the moment.

column1: You look at the apple cores, and quickly decide they are of no use to you.  Besides, they look disgusting.
column2: 你看著蘋果核，迅速決定它們對你沒用。而且它們看起來噁心得要死。

column1: You are in the Meeps' Peep.  The colorful, furry Meeps timidly pop out of their holes from time to time.
column2: 你在咪普的窩裡。色彩繽紛、毛茸茸的咪普們不時膽小地冒出洞口。

column1: The baby Meep sure is cute.
column2: 小咪普真的很可愛。

column1: All of the Meeps are hiding deep within their holes.  Your vicious attack has them terrified.
column2: 所有咪普都躲在洞裡深處。你的凶悍攻擊嚇得它們要死。

column1: You realize that you have never seen more than one green Meep at a time.  Perhaps there is only one.
column2: 你意識到你從沒同時見過一隻以上的綠咪普。或許只有一隻。

column1: There seems to be an abundant supply of blue and purple Meeps.  If only they'd stay still for a moment, perhaps you could count them.
column2: There seems to be an abundant supply of blue and purple Meeps.  If only they'd stay still for a moment, perhaps you could count them.

column1: You see blue Meeps, purple Meeps, and occasionally a green Meep.  They seem to be very shy; whenever you approach one, it hides under its rock.
column2: 你看見藍咪普、紫咪普，偶爾還有綠咪普。它們看起來超害羞；你一靠近，它就躲到石頭底下。

column1: Large, heavy-looking rocks cover the many holes.  The Meeps must be stronger than they look to lift these rocks so easily.
column2: 大塊沉重的石頭蓋著許多洞。咪普們要夠強悍才能這麼輕鬆搬起這些石頭。

column1: You keep seeing differently-colored Meeps come out of each hole.  They either lead to large underground caverns, or they are all connected under the earth.
column2: 你不斷看見不同顏色的咪普從每個洞冒出來。它們要嘛通向大型地下洞穴，要嘛全部在地底連通。

column1: The trees and bushes look much like those you've seen elsewhere in the valley.
column2: 樹木和灌木看起來跟你在山谷其他地方見過的很像。

column1: The mountains are very steep here.
column2: 這邊的山峰陡得要命。

column1: The meadow is covered with a light carpet of grass, broken up by the Meep holes.
column2: 草地被薄薄的草皮覆蓋著，中間散佈著咪普的洞。

column1: There is a wad of bright green fur on the ground.
column2: 地上有一團亮綠色的毛皮。

column1: The Meeps have brightly-colored, soft-looking fur.
column2: 咪普長著色彩繽紛、軟呼呼的毛皮。

column1: It looks like a Spell Scroll.
column2: 看起來像一卷魔法捲軸。

column1: The scroll is blank now, but once contained the Detect Magic spell.
column2: The scroll is blank now, but once contained the Detect Magic spell.

column1: You can't talk to any of the Meeps.  They are all hiding deep within their holes, terrified after your vicious attack.
column2: 你沒辦法跟任何咪普說話。它們都躲在洞裡深處，被你的凶悍攻擊嚇得要死。

column1: Hey, just ask me about anything you want to know.
column2: 「嘿，有什麼你想知道的儘管問我。」

column1: You don't see any Meeps to talk to at the moment.
column2: You don't see any Meeps to talk to at the moment.

column1: We use rocks for doors.  They keep us dry and warm.
column2: We use rocks for doors.  They keep us dry and warm.

column1: We are happy Meeps, living in our happy holes.  Don't worry.  Be happy!
column2: 「我們是快樂的咪普，住在我們快樂的洞裡。別擔心。要開心！」

column1: Hey, go ahead, take it. It's yours.
column2: Hey, go ahead, take it. It's yours.

column1: Hey, like fur is good stuff, keeps us warm.  Mine's the best -- it's like green, you know?
column2: 「嘿，咦，毛皮是好東西啊，能讓我們暖和。我的最讚──是綠色的，你知道吧？」

column1: Gee, boss, I really don't know much about that at all.  Hey, sorry.
column2: Gee, boss, I really don't know much about that at all.  Hey, sorry.

column1: Why, that's me!
column2: Why, that's me!

column1: Man, those rocks are tough!
column2: Man, those rocks are tough!

column1: You hear squeaky muttering from beneath the ground.  It seems the Meeps are having quite a discussion about you.
column2: 你聽見地底傳來吱吱嘎嘎的碎碎念。看來咪普們正在熱烈討論你呢。

column1: Hiya, hiya.  Nice to seeya 'gain.
column2: Hiya, hiya.  Nice to seeya 'gain.

column1: Hiya, hiya.  Really pleased to meetcha.
column2: Hiya, hiya.  Really pleased to meetcha.

column1: Hey, there ya go.  Hope it helps ya.
column2: Hey, there ya go.  Hope it helps ya.

column1: Hey, sorry I couldn't help ya.
column2: Hey, sorry I couldn't help ya.

column1: Oh, you want %s?  I think I have %s somewhere around here.
column2: Oh, you want %s?  I think I have %s somewhere around here.

column1: Gosh, I don't seem to have anything like that left.  Really sorry 'bout that.
column2: Gosh, I don't seem to have anything like that left.  Really sorry 'bout that.

column1: You pick up the pile of soft green fur and carefully place it in your pack.
column2: You pick up the pile of soft green fur and carefully place it in your pack.

column1: You pick up the Spell Scroll.  As the magical runes fade, you find you now know how to cast the "Detect Magic" spell.
column2: 你撿起魔法捲軸。當符文褪去時，你發現自己現在知道怎麼施展「偵測魔法」法術了。

column1: The magical runes fade from the paper before your eye can focus on them.
column2: The magical runes fade from the paper before your eye can focus on them.

column2: Don't be greedy.  You already have some.

column2: 你怎麼可能得到那個?

column2: There is a ring of mushrooms on the northwest side of the clearing.

column1: There aren't any of those around.
column2: There aren't any of those around.

column2: You see trees and brush.

column1: I'll bet you're sorry you said that.
column2: 我打賭你現在後悔說那句話了。

column1: For such wimpy-looking creatures, those fairies sure can play rough!  You never danced so hard in your entire life, which is now over.
column2: For such wimpy-looking creatures, those fairies sure can play rough!  You never danced so hard in your entire life, which is now over.

column2: You eat a few of the lovely mushrooms.

column2: 你摘下一把小蘑菇，小心翼翼地放進背包裡。

column2: 你小心翼翼地把粉末放進空瓶子裡。

column2: 你體會到你需要像空瓶子這樣的容器來裝這些妖精塵。

column2: As the fairy dust sifts through your fingers, you realize that you needed something to put it into.

column1: Wow!  That was pretty wild!  It's probably not a good idea to eat too many more of these mushrooms, though.
column2: 哇!那相當狂野!不過吃更多這些蘑菇可能不是個好主意。

column2: The objects on the ground are a brigand's shield and spear.

column2: The dead brigand's shield is not worth your while.

column2: You are using the old archery target for dagger practice.

column2: The only daggers around here are the ones you've thrown at the target.

column2: There are no useable spears near you.

column2: Some marauding beast must have taken the brigand's body away.

column2: You see nothing like that here.

column2: The wall and buildings of Spielburg can be seen over the heavy brush.  An old target leans against the town wall.

column2: You see a couple of hard-looking characters talking.  One looks like a thief; the other appears to be a fighter of some sort.

column1: So what's this about, anyway?
column2: 這到底是怎麼回事？

column2: 那位高高在上的傢伙開始懷疑我們了。

column2: 她怎麼了？

column1: Seems the hero" wandering around here has her leery.  She thinks he's going to go for the gold on her head."
column2: Seems the hero" wandering around here has her leery.  She thinks he's going to go for the gold on her head."

column2: 這跟我們有什麼關係？

column2: 她一直在問太多關於我們的問題。

column1: And the laughing jackass' eyeing me.  I had to sneak out.
column2: And the laughing jackass' eyeing me.  I had to sneak out.

column2: 不，我們先等那傢伙離開。

column1: Where the bouncer hops around.  Ya got your key still?

column1: Yeah.
column2: 是的。

column2: Don't lose it.  I got the only other one.  Yull haveta search the rock for the keyhole.  It's hidden good.  And remember the 'word'.

column2: What Word?

column2: 那個『密碼』讓你進去，這樣佛瑞德就會消失。

column2: Oh yeah, sure.

column1: What is it?
column2: What is it?

column2: 你以為我是個傻瓜或什麼的？

column2: Say the 'word' before ya open the door or ya might make Fred mad.

column1: Hiden Goseke.
column2: 希登·格塞克。

column1: Hiden Goseke.  Got it.
column2: 希登·格塞克。明白了。

column1: I gotta get back before the Chief misses me.  Be back in a bit.
column2: 我得在頭兒發現我之前回去。等一下就回來。

column1: Hiden Goseke.  See ya.
column2: 希登·格塞克。回見。

column1: Done in by poisoned daggers and desperate desperadoes!  Try to avoid annoying that deadly duo next time.
column2: 被毒匕首和孤注一擲的亡命漢幹掉!下次別惹那對致命組合。

column1: Hey, watchu doin' here? Spyin', I'll wager!

column1: Done in by daggers and a desperate desperado!  Try to avoid annoying that deadly duo next time.
column2: 被匕首和孤注一擲的亡命漢幹掉!下次別惹那對致命組合。

column2: You're too close to the target for it to be a challange.

column2: You have no daggers to throw.

column2: You can't get any daggers.

column2: For a moment there, you thought you saw something.  But nothing is there now.

column2: 「你需要成為森林的朋友。不要傷害溫和的生物或植物。用你所有的感官去感受樹木和野生地方的魔法。」

column2: 「漫步其中吧,並學習。然後你也會與森林和諧相處。」

column2: The stag looks more surprised than hurt.

column2: 白鹿被你的行動嚇到了。

column2: 你被迫承認你對樹精撒謊了。你沒有魔法種子。

column2: She tells you:  "It's not nice to fool Mother Nature."

column2: 給我帶來一粒來自北邊的孢子噴發繡線菊的種子，這樣我可以在別處種植它，以保護這些稀有的魔法植物。

column1: Thus will you become a true friend of the forest.

column1: Please.  Just answer my question.
column2: Please.  Just answer my question.

column2: The rocks to the north look too steep.

column1: Perhaps the farmer has dates in his field.
column2: Perhaps the farmer has dates in his field.

column1: I beg your pardon?
column2: I beg your pardon?

column2: 不會，但她是個真正的難相處傢伙。

column2: 別讓自己出醜。

column2: 當你開始萌生暴力的念頭時…

column1: You think better of burning up the woods.
column2: You think better of burning up the woods.

column1: She's beyond your reach.
column2: 她超出你的範圍。

column1: You cannot look at what you cannot see.
column2: You cannot look at what you cannot see.

column1: There is a rock wall northwest of the large oak tree.
column2: There is a rock wall northwest of the large oak tree.

column1: The majestic white stag is an outstanding example of the beauty of the animal kingdom.
column2: 莊嚴的白鹿是動物王國美麗的傑出典範。

column1: You see no such animal.
column2: You see no such animal.

column1: There is a large, slightly glowing acorn on the ground.

column1: There are acorns here and there on the ground.

column2: The forest becomes so lush and thick that it is difficult to see far.

column1: The brush and undergrowth has formed an impassable thicket.
column2: The brush and undergrowth has formed an impassable thicket.

column2: You see thick brush, rocks, and heavy woods to the north.  A large oak tree dominates the view.

column2: You see a clearing back the way you came.

column1: Most of the bushes around here seem to be bramble and are very thorny.
column2: 這裡周圍的大多數灌木似乎是荊棘，非常有刺。

column2: 你跟著白鹿進入森林的角落。你感覺彷彿整個森林的眼睛都在注視著你。

column1: Again, you feel a sense of closeness with nature.
column2: 你再次感到與自然相近的感覺。

column1: You watch the stag, fascinated with his grace and beauty.  There is something special about this place.
column2: 你看著白鹿,被它的優雅和美麗迷住了。這個地方有什麼特別的。

column1: What a beautiful animal!
column2: 多麼美麗的動物!

column1: Have you brought the seed I requested?
column2: 「你帶來我要求的種子了嗎?」

column1: Well, Hero-to-be!  Are you yet a friend of the woods?
column2: 「好吧,未來的英雄!你是森林的朋友嗎?」

column1: I am the Dryad, keeper of the woods.  Are you one with the woods?
column2: I am the Dryad, keeper of the woods.  Are you one with the woods?

column2: 你看到一個橡樹果慢慢地掉到地上。

column1: You drop the seed into the Dryad's limbs.
column2: 你把種子掉進樹精的枝幹中。

column1: You tell the Dryad:  "No amount of persuasion could convince the flowers to relinquish their seed easily, so I was forced to hack my way to the seed with my weapon."
column2: 你告訴樹精:「無論多少勸說都無法輕易說服花朵放棄它們的種子,所以我被迫用武器砍出一條路到達種子。」

column1: Now this will boldly grow where none has grown before!
column2: 「現在它將大膽地在無人種植過的地方生長!」

column1: Heed now my words, friend of the forest, and heed them well.
column2: 「現在就要聽我的話,森林的朋友,要聽清楚。」

column1: Friend, you must know that there is an evil in this valley which perverts the ways of nature.  The prophecy says that a hero will bring a young human from out this darkness.
column2: Friend, you must know that there is an evil in this valley which perverts the ways of nature.  The prophecy says that a hero will bring a young human from out this darkness.

column1: If you are the one to accomplish this task, there is a potion to break enchantments which you must have the Healer make.
column2: If you are the one to accomplish this task, there is a potion to break enchantments which you must have the Healer make.

column1: You must gather these ingredients:
column2: 「你必須收集這些材料:」

column1: Flowers from Erana's Peace,
column2: 「伊瑞娜的安寧之地的花朵,」

column1: Green Fur,
column2: 「綠色的毛皮,」

column1: Fairy Dust,
column2: 「妖精之塵,」

column1: a Magic Acorn,
column2: 「一個魔法橡實,」

column1: and Flying Water.
column2: 「和飛行之水。」

column1: Farewell, friend.  I must return to my concentration.  May the forest forever surround you.
column2: 「再見了,朋友。我必須回去冥想。願森林永遠環繞著你。」

column1: You pick it up and eat it.
column2: 你拿起它並吃了它。

column1: The acorn appears too ordinary.  You drop it back on the ground.
column2: 那顆橡實看起來太普通了。你把它掉回地上。

column1: You did WHAT???
column2: 你做了什麼？？？

column1: You have destroyed the plant that was to be preserved!
column2: 「你摧毀了本應被保護的植物!」

column1: As you have destroyed, so shall you now become!
column2: 「正如你所摧毀的,你現在也將變成這樣!」

column1: Well, it looks like the Dryad really made you eat dirt.  You're the spitting image of a Spirea plant, and you'll be hanging around for a long, long time.
column2: 嗯,看起來樹精真的讓你吃土了。你簡直就是繡球花的翻版,你會在那邊掛很久很久。

column1: You have attempted to harm a free creature of the forest!
column2: 「你試圖傷害森林的自由生物!」

column1: You shall now become a part of the forest!
column2: 「你現在將成為森林的一部分!」

column2: 「你濫用了你的技能,哦,法師!你在我的森林上使用了火焰鏢。」

column1: You must be made less dangerous.
column2: 「你必須變得不那麼危險。」

column1: You dare to harm the Dryad of this forest!  You must be made less dangerous.
column2: You dare to harm the Dryad of this forest!  You must be made less dangerous.

column1: You are staggered by the Dryad's spell.  She appears to enjoy having lots of bucks.
column2: 你被樹精的咒語震撼。她好像喜歡有一堆錢。

column1: You sleep comfortably and well in this quiet and protected corner of the woods.
column2: You sleep comfortably and well in this quiet and protected corner of the woods.

column1: Your spell has no effect.
column2: 你的魔法沒有效果。

column1: The stag seems to be magically protected.
column2: The stag seems to be magically protected.

column1: You don't see a stag here.
column2: You don't see a stag here.

column1: The beautiful white stag is foraging for food.
column2: 美麗的白鹿正在覓食。

column1: The white stag takes a mighty leap.
column2: 白鹿縱身一躍。

column1: You seem to have startled the white stag.
column2: 你似乎嚇到了白鹿。

column1: You seem to have caused an Antwerp POPulation EXPLOSION!
column2: 你似乎導致了一場安特衛普人口爆炸!

column1: You see no Antwerps here.
column2: You see no Antwerps here.

column1: The bouncing baby Antwerps are all so cute, you can't bring yourself to interfere with their playing.
column2: 跳躍著的小安特衛普都很可愛，你捨不得打擾它們玩耍。

column1: Looking up, you can see a small, blue dot in the sky, getting bigger... and bigger... and BIGGER!
column2: 向上看,你可以看到天空中一個小的藍點,越來越大…還有更大…還有更大!

column1: Too late!
column2: 太遲了！

column1: There's no time for that!
column2: 沒有時間做那個!

column1: Where to? There is no escaping the hurtling blue blob.
column2: Where to? There is no escaping the hurtling blue blob.

column1: You're obviously in no shape to continue the game.
column2: 你顯然沒有體力繼續遊戲了。

column1: There is a roaring sound off to the east.
column2: There is a roaring sound off to the east.

column1: The hollow log looks somberly back with it's single giant eye and you know at once that nothing is to be gained by investigating this gaunt relic of a more vertical past.
column2: The hollow log looks somberly back with it's single giant eye and you know at once that nothing is to be gained by investigating this gaunt relic of a more vertical past.

column1: You'll have to climb down to do that.
column2: You'll have to climb down to do that.

column1: A river plunges for more than a hundred feet down the face of a cliff.  A doorway has been built into the side of the cliff.
column2: A river plunges for more than a hundred feet down the face of a cliff.  A doorway has been built into the side of the cliff.

column1: The water seems to be trying to fly as it leaps from the mountain above.
column2: 水似乎在從上面的山上躍下時試圖飛起。

column1: You see slick, vertical cliff walls.
column2: You see slick, vertical cliff walls.

column1: The water is white as it splashes against the rocks with great force and purpose and disappears into a narrow canyon to the south.
column2: The water is white as it splashes against the rocks with great force and purpose and disappears into a narrow canyon to the south.

column1: The grass is green with the freshness of new spring.
column2: The grass is green with the freshness of new spring.

column1: There is a narrow ledge in front of the wide door.
column2: 寬門前有一個狹窄的岩架。

column1: You don't see a ladder here.
column2: 你在這裡看不到梯子。

column1: The door is built into the face of the mountainside.  You suspect that the person who lives behind it treasures privacy.
column2: 門被建造在山邊的表面。你懷疑住在它後面的人珍視隱私。

column1: It's a large door.  It must open pretty wide.
column2: 這是一扇大門。它一定開得相當寬。

column1: you don't have a flask
column2: you don't have a flask

column1: The water comes from melting snow.  This early in the season, it's much too cold to swim.
column2: The water comes from melting snow.  This early in the season, it's much too cold to swim.

column1: The door is securely locked.
column2: The door is securely locked.

column1: The door is so securely locked that it defies your abilities to unlock it.
column2: The door is so securely locked that it defies your abilities to unlock it.

column1: It's a little hard to knock on the door from where you're standing.
column2: 從你站的地方敲門有點困難。

column1: You're in front of the door already.  You don't need to climb any farther.
column2: You're in front of the door already.  You don't need to climb any farther.

column1: As you prepare your spell...
column2: 當你準備你的魔法…

column1: The only thing you can open here is the door, and you're not skilled enough to do that.
column2: 你唯一能在這裡打開的東西是門,而你沒有足夠的技能來做那個。

column1: Wheeee!
column2: 呼!

column1: Wow!
column2: 哇!

column1: The roar of the waterfall fills your ears, and the cold spray dampens your face as you approach.
column2: 瀑布的轟鳴聲填滿了你的耳朵,當你靠近時,冷的噴霧潮濕了你的臉。

column1: RAP!
column2: 敲擊!

column1: The rock makes a sharp sound as it hits the door.
column2: 岩石撞到門時發出尖銳的聲音。

column1: Missed
column2: Missed

column1: All right, already!
column2: 「好吧,已經!」

column1: Is someone there?
column2: 「有人嗎?」

column1: You again?  Either come up, or go away.   I really doesn't cares which.
column2: 「又是你?要麼上來,要麼滾開。我真的不在乎哪一個。」

column1: Oh, 'ello.  Come right up.
column2: 「哦,嘿。直接上來。」

column1: Just climb the ladder.
column2: 「就爬梯子吧。」

column1: You take a drink of water from the icy mountain river.  It refreshes you.
column2: 你從冰冷的山河喝了一口水。它使你恢復活力。

column1: You take another drink of water.  You were thirsty!
column2: 你又喝了一口水。你很渴!

column1: BOY!  You must have really been thirsty!
column2: 天哪!你一定真的很渴!

column1: You fill an empty flask with crystal-clear water from the waterfall.
column2: 你用瀑布的清澈的水填滿了一個空的瓶子。

column1: Flat...you feel very, very flat.
column2: Flat...you feel very, very flat.

column1: And dead...you feel dead, too.  In your weakened condition, you succumbed to a mild-mannered hermit's propensity for Tarzan imitations.  Back up and play it again, Sam.
column2: And dead...you feel dead, too.  In your weakened condition, you succumbed to a mild-mannered hermit's propensity for Tarzan imitations.  Back up and play it again, Sam.

column1: That hermit seems to know his "Trigger" spells pretty well.  He sure pulled the trigger on you (not to mention the plug) by teleporting you to the top of the falls without a barrel.
column2: 那隱士看起來很懂「觸發術」。他肯定對你發動了觸發（更別提直接結束了），把你傳送到瀑布頂端卻沒給桶子。

column1: You knock three times.
column2: 你敲了三下。

column1: Just a minute!  Just a minute!
column2: Just a minute!  Just a minute!

column1: The straw is relatively fresh and looks more comfortable to lie upon than the rocks.
column2: 稻草相對新鮮，看起來比石頭舒服多了。

column1: Yes, what you see is a table.
column2: 是的，你看到的就是桌子。

column1: You see a chair.
column2: You see a chair.

column1: You see a cot.
column2: 你看到一張簡易床。

column1: A single candle sits upon the table.
column2: 一根蠟燭孤獨地擺在桌上。

column1: You see the shaggiest little man (you think it's a man, anyway) you've ever seen.
column2: 你看到一個毛髮蓬亂的小個子（你想應該是個男人啦）。

column1: Come in, come in.  Good to see you again.   Get's kind o' tiresome sitten here an' thinken by meself all the time.
column2: 「進來進來。很高興又見到你。一個人坐在這裡想東想西有點煩人。」

column1: The cave is cold and clammy and it smells like mildew and wet dog hair in here!
column2: The cave is cold and clammy and it smells like mildew and wet dog hair in here!

column1: The walls are covered with phosphorescent fungus.
column2: The walls are covered with phosphorescent fungus.

column1: The phosphorescent fungus glows eerily in the cave's dim light.
column2: The phosphorescent fungus glows eerily in the cave's dim light.

column1: It's on the table.
column2: 它在桌上。

column1: Me guest bed is the straw in the corner over there. You're welcome to stay the night since you've shared your rations. When you want to sleep, just say so.
column2: 「我的客用床是那邊角落的稻草。既然你分享了口糧，歡迎你留下睡一晚。想睡的時候就說一聲。」

column1: Me guest bed is the straw in the corner over there. You're welcome to come back and sleep tonight since you've shared your rations.
column2: Me guest bed is the straw in the corner over there. You're welcome to come back and sleep tonight since you've shared your rations.

column1: You can't share what you don't have.
column2: 你沒有的東西無法分享。

column1: You can't sleep in this old rickety chair.  Get up and sleep on the bed of straw.
column2: 你沒法在這把老舊搖搖晃晃的椅子上睡覺。起來睡稻草床吧。

column1: Get closer to the bed of straw.
column2: Get closer to the bed of straw.

column1: You need to get closer to the chair.
column2: You need to get closer to the chair.

column1: You are already sitting... on your brain, perhaps?
column2: You are already sitting... on your brain, perhaps?

column1: You're not sitting.
column2: You're not sitting.

column1: The 'ermit wouldn't like that.
column2: The 'ermit wouldn't like that.

column1: You should wait until the hermit is asleep to check his place out.
column2: 你應該等隱士睡著才去查看他的地方。

column1: Despite your intentions, you can't bring yourself to spend any more time playing with the talkative 'Enry.
column2: Despite your intentions, you can't bring yourself to spend any more time playing with the talkative 'Enry.

column1: You spend an hour at the game while 'Enry talks.  You've obviously made his day.
column2: You spend an hour at the game while 'Enry talks.  You've obviously made his day.

column1: You should sit down first.
column2: You should sit down first.

column1: The scroll vanishes even as you read the magical runes upon it.   You now have the knowledge to cast a "Trigger" spell.
column2: 當你讀著上面的魔法符文時，捲軸消失了。你現在掌握了施展「觸發術」的知識。

column1: You see no scroll.
column2: You see no scroll.

column1: You can detect a faint aura of magic here.
column2: 你能感受到這裡有微弱的魔法光暈。

column1: There is no need to use magic to open anything here.
column2: 這裡沒有什麼需要魔法才能打開的。

column1: It's not polite to steal from your 'airy little 'ost.
column2: It's not polite to steal from your 'airy little 'ost.

column1: You seem to have made the 'ermit un'appy.
column2: 看來你惹得這隱士很不開心。

column1: Me guest bed is the straw in the corner over there. You're welcome to stay if you share some rations and play some cribbage.
column2: 「我的客用床是那邊角落的稻草。要是你願意分享口糧和我玩幾盤牌戲，歡迎你留下。」

column1: If you be wanting to learn the spell, I'll be pleased to share it to you.  It's just getting molding in me 'iding place.
column2: If you be wanting to learn the spell, I'll be pleased to share it to you.  It's just getting molding in me 'iding place.

column1: I think you be 'avin too little magic for it to be any use to you.
column2: 「我想你的魔法太弱，對你沒用。」

column1: Do you want it?
column2: 「你要嗎？」

column1: Oh well, maybe sumone else can use it sumtime.
column2: 「嗯，或許別人以後用得上。」

column1: You already 'ave me only scroll.
column2: 「你已經有我唯一的捲軸了。」

column1: Yes, I still have me magic scroll, but you didn't seem to want it. 'Ave you changed your mind?
column2: 「是啊，我還有我的魔法捲軸，但你似乎不想要。你改主意了嗎？」

column1: Let's see.  'Ow does Erasmus do it?  Oh, yes.
column2: 「讓我想想。伊拉斯謨怎麼做的？喔，對了。」

column1: 'E summons it with a Trigger.  'Ere goes!
column2: 「他用觸發術召喚它。來吧！」

column1: There you 'as it.
column2: 「看吧，就這樣。」

column1: I don't 'ave much, but wot I 'ave, I 'ave, an' t'isn't for sale.
column2: I don't 'ave much, but wot I 'ave, I 'ave, an' t'isn't for sale.

column1: Well, see'en as I don't get out much, I don't know much.
column2: Well, see'en as I don't get out much, I don't know much.

column1: If you be ever needen a place to stay the night and you're in the area, I could be willen to put you up for the night for sum rations and a couple o' games o' cribbage.
column2: 「要是你以後需要地方睡覺又在這一帶，我樂意為了幾份口糧和幾盤牌戲讓你睡一晚。」

column1: After having traded a ration, which the 'ermit promptly swallowed, and spending a rather boring hour of cribbage and listening to 'Enry chatter, you hit the hay.
column2: After having traded a ration, which the 'ermit promptly swallowed, and spending a rather boring hour of cribbage and listening to 'Enry chatter, you hit the hay.

column1: Unfortunately, since the fungus on the walls shines at night so the room doesn't get dark, and the fact the hermit talks most of the night, you only get five hours rest.
column2: 很遺憾，由於牆上的真菌在夜間發光讓房間保持明亮，加上隱士幾乎整晚都在講話，你只睡了5小時。

column1: You can't sleep here, you got's no rations to give me.
column2: 「你沒法睡這裡，你沒有口糧給我。」

column1: By golly, It's %s!
column2: By golly, It's %s!

column1: Now that the big Antwerp has split, this corner of the forest seems strangely quiet.
column2: 現在大安特衛普已經分裂，森林的這個角落變得奇異地安靜。

column1: You look around and see rocks and grass, but no Antwerp.
column2: 你環顧四周，看到岩石和草，但沒有安特衛普。

column1: You see rocks, grass and an Antwerp.
column2: 你看到岩石、草和一隻安特衛普。

column1: The only Antwerp known in these parts has split... into parts.
column2: 這一帶唯一的安特衛普已經分裂…成碎片了。

column1: The Antwerp seems to have flown the coop.
column2: 安特衛普似乎逃脫了。

column1: Antwerps are on the endangered species list.  They are rarely seen.
column2: Antwerps are on the endangered species list.  They are rarely seen.

column1: The rocks were left here by some receding glacier.
column2: 這些岩石是被某座退縮的冰河遺留下來的。

column1: There is a narrow cave entrance among the rocks.
column2: 岩石中有一個狹窄的洞穴入口。

column1: You find a keyhole concealed in a crack in the rock.
column2: 你在岩石裂縫中發現了隱藏的鑰孔。

column1: You do not find anything nearby.
column2: You do not find anything nearby.

column1: The grass is luscious, just the thing for hungry herbivores.
column2: 草很茂盛，正是饑餓的草食動物喜歡的。

column1: The way is impassable.  Sheer rock cliffs rise to serious heights.
column2: 無法通行。陡峭的岩石懸崖高聳到驚人的高度。

column1: The forest extends to the east and north.
column2: 森林向東和向北延伸。

column1: Antwerps aren't dazzled easily.
column2: 安特衛普不容易被眩目。

column1: You aren't close enough to a lock.
column2: You aren't close enough to a lock.

column1: You have no weapon with which to fight the Antwerp.
column2: You have no weapon with which to fight the Antwerp.

column1: However, you bravely attack the bouncing beast with your bare hands.
column2: However, you bravely attack the bouncing beast with your bare hands.

column1: The only Antwerp known to these parts, split ...into parts.
column2: The only Antwerp known to these parts, split ...into parts.

column1: He's on a diet.
column2: He's on a diet.

column1: You don't see any locks nearby.
column2: 你附近看不到任何鎖。

column1: The lock in the rock clicks open.
column2: 岩石上的鎖發出喀噠一聲打開了。

column1: You'd have a much easier time of this if you had the key.
column2: 有鑰匙的話會容易得多。

column1: Ah, got it!  The lock in the rock clicks open.
column2: 啊，成功了！岩石上的鎖發出喀噠一聲打開了。

column1: The lock is beyond your present skill.  It might help if you had the key.
column2: 這把鎖超出你現在的能力。有鑰匙的話可能會有幫助。

column1: ... Or at least a better set of tools.
column2: …或至少一套更好的工具。

column1: You are not strong enough yet to open the rock door.
column2: 你還不夠強大無法打開石門。

column1: Despite your mightiest efforts, the rock does not move.
column2: 儘管你盡力了，岩石還是沒有動。

column1: The rock door has already been opened.
column2: 石門已經打開了。

column1: You hear the sound of someone...or something...moving deeper into the cave to let you pass.
column2: You hear the sound of someone...or something...moving deeper into the cave to let you pass.

column1: Ok, you say Hiden Goseke.
column2: Ok, you say Hiden Goseke.

column1: Go ahead. If you dare.
column2: Go ahead. If you dare.

column1: That's funny.  It bounced right off.
column2: 真有趣。它直接彈開了。

column1: By golly, it's an Antwerp!
column2: By golly, it's an Antwerp!

column1: The Antwerp plays rough.
column2: The Antwerp plays rough.

column1: The old ticker just couldn't keep going.  Maybe you shouldn't have missed the annual visit with your local Healer.
column2: The old ticker just couldn't keep going.  Maybe you shouldn't have missed the annual visit with your local Healer.

column1: Cockily, you loosen up to fight.
column2: 你傲慢地放鬆身體準備打鬥。

column1: Confident and loose, you approach the Antwerp.
column2: 你自信而放鬆地走近安特衛普。

column1: Holy Mackerel!
column2: 「天哪！」

column1: Your spell has unlocked the lock on the rock, but it is not yet powerful enough to open the rock door.
column2: 你的法術打開了岩石上的鎖，但還不夠強大到打開石門。

column1: You seem to have scared the Antwerp with your behavior.
column2: 你的行為似乎嚇到了安特衛普。

column1: You see your own reflection in the crystal-clear waters.
column2: 你在清澈透明的水中看到自己的倒影。

column1: You see a lovely waterfall in the distance, feeding the lake with ice cold mountain water.
column2: 你看到遠處有一座美麗的瀑布，用冰冷的山泉餵養著湖泊。

column1: You see a flock of geese as it passes overhead.
column2: You see a flock of geese as it passes overhead.

column1: You see the reflection of a flock of geese as it passes overhead.
column2: You see the reflection of a flock of geese as it passes overhead.

column1: It must be your imagination.  What would a submarine be doing in a fantasy adventure?  Perhaps you'd rather be playing `Code Name: Iceman' -- it has a submarine.
column2: It must be your imagination.  What would a submarine be doing in a fantasy adventure?  Perhaps you'd rather be playing `Code Name: Iceman' -- it has a submarine.

column1: You don't want to do that.  A walking pace is more appropriate for enjoying the majestic beauty of this scene.
column2: 你不想那樣做。漫步的步調更適合欣賞這場景的雄偉之美。

column1: Swimming in the Spiegelsee is a bad idea.  The lake is fed from mountain run-off, and the water is ice cold.
column2: 在史畢格爾湖中游泳是個壞主意。這個湖由山區徑流餵養，水冰冷刺骨。

column1: The lake water tastes pretty good, if a bit flat.
column2: 湖水嚐起來相當不錯，只是有點沒味道。

column1: ... but this is not the water you want.
column2: ……但這不是你要的水。

column1: You fill a flask with the clear lake water.
column2: 你用清澈的湖水裝滿一個瓶子。

column1: You don't have anything in which to carry the water.
column2: You don't have anything in which to carry the water.

column1: It's the beautiful Spiegelsee (Mirror Lake).  You pause for some peaceful reflection.
column2: It's the beautiful Spiegelsee (Mirror Lake).  You pause for some peaceful reflection.

column1: There is a strong odor of decay and other more unpleasant smells filling the air.  A pile of something unpleasant lies near the center of this cave.
column2: There is a strong odor of decay and other more unpleasant smells filling the air.  A pile of something unpleasant lies near the center of this cave.

column1: You can watch the water ooze down the sides of the stalactites and drip to the ground.
column2: 你可以看著水從鐘乳石的側面滲出，滴到地面。

column1: You can watch the water drop down from the stalactites and ooze down the sides of the stalagmites.
column2: 你可以看著水從鐘乳石滴下，從石筍的側面滲下。

column1: The water has been polluted by various other disgusting liquids.
column2: The water has been polluted by various other disgusting liquids.

column1: It looks as if someone has piled up bones, bits of armor, and things you can't quite make out and probably don't want to know about. It smells disgusting.
column2: It looks as if someone has piled up bones, bits of armor, and things you can't quite make out and probably don't want to know about. It smells disgusting.

column1: A mean-looking Troll looms before you, a snarl on his lips.
column2: 一隻看起來兇惡的巨魔聳立在你面前，嘴角帶著咆哮。

column1: The Troll lies dying upon the slimy floor of the cave.
column2: 巨魔躺在洞穴粘滑的地板上奄奄一息。

column1: A dead Troll lies in a pool of blue blood.
column2: 一隻死巨魔躺在藍色血液的水池中。

column1: You see no such creature here.
column2: 你在這裡看不到這樣的生物。

column1: You have lost your sense of direction, as the cave passage twists around.
column2: 當洞穴通道蜿蜒時，你已經失去了方向感。

column1: The rocks look slick and are slimy to the touch.
column2: 岩石看起來光滑且摸起來有粘液。

column1: The light from the fungus is eerie and vaguely unpleasant.
column2: 真菌發出的光線詭異且隱隱令人不快。

column1: The only entrance to this cave is the one you came through.
column2: 這個洞穴的唯一入口就是你進來的那個。

column1: You can't do that.
column2: You can't do that.

column1: You need to get closer to the dead Troll.
column2: You need to get closer to the dead Troll.

column1: The password is for the hidden passage, not the troll's home.
column2: The password is for the hidden passage, not the troll's home.

column1: Get closer to the pile.
column2: Get closer to the pile.

column1: The dead Troll's huge club is much too heavy for you to lift.
column2: 死去的巨魔的巨大棍棒對你來說太重了，抬不起來。

column1: You don't need it.
column2: You don't need it.

column1: This Troll looks as though he'd rather eat YOU!
column2: This Troll looks as though he'd rather eat YOU!

column1: You hear the constant "drip... drip..." of water.
column2: 你聽到水的不斷「滴……滴……」聲。

column1: You hear the constant "drip... drip..." of water, and the unmistakeable sound of something breathing nearby.
column2: You hear the constant "drip... drip..." of water, and the unmistakeable sound of something breathing nearby.

column1: There is no magic in this cave.
column2: 這個洞穴裡沒有魔法。

column1: This was not the ending you had in mind when you read the manual on "How to be a Hero".  Either you just aren't ready to take on this situation, or there's got to be a better way to accomplish your mission.

column2: 你聽到跳跳盒上的門栓被打開時發出的輕響。

column2: The flying Wallendas have nothing on you.

column1: No time for that again (although it WAS fun).  Just get the heck outta here.
column2: 現在沒時間再玩了(雖然確實很好玩)。趕快逃出這鬼地方。

column1: You're not in a good spot.
column2: You're not in a good spot.

column1: It's too late for that.
column2: It's too late for that.

column1: Brigands over all!  Egotistical bunch, aren't they?
column2: Brigands over all!  Egotistical bunch, aren't they?

column1: The rope holds up the chandelier.  It's used to lower the chandelier so that it can be lit.
column2: 繩子吊著吊燈。它被用來放低吊燈好點燃它。

column2: 看起來很堅固的燭台。

column1: Chandelier, used to hold up the rope.
column2: 吊燈，用來吊著繩子。

column1: You close and bar the door.
column2: 你關上門並用木條封住了。

column1: You're hopelessly outnumbered.  You should have left this room when you had a chance.
column2: 你被絕望地包圍。你應該在還有機會時離開這個房間。

column1: It looks like the talking is over.
column2: It looks like the talking is over.

column1: No more talking, my friend.  Let's have fun!
column2: 「別再囉唆了，我的朋友。我們來玩樂!」

column1: Now??
column2: 現在？？

column1: ME sees you preparing your spell and...
column2: 「我」看到你在準備法術……

column1: You're too busy.
column2: 你太忙了。

column1: ME sees you preparing to throw and...
column2: ME sees you preparing to throw and...

column1: Why would you want to throw that?
column2: 你為什麼想丟那個?

column1: You know me?  Then perhaps you have come to help.  Perhaps you are able to take the child from the band.
column2: 「你認識我？那也許你是來幫忙的。也許你能把小孩從這幫人手中救出來。」

column1: Be more specific, my friend.
column2: 「請說得更具體一點，朋友。」

column1: Talking is a speciality of those who know not what they talk about.
column2: 「談話是那些不知道自己在說什麼的人的專長。」

column1: You know Elsa?  Then perhaps you have come to help.  Perhaps you are able to take the child from the band.
column2: 「你認識艾爾莎?那你可能是來幫忙的。也許你有辦法把孩子從山賊手中救出。」

column1: After you have explained what you know about Elsa, Yorick speaks:
column2: 在你解釋了你對艾爾莎的了解後，尤瑞克說話了：

column1: It's about time that someone else helped Elsa before it's too late!  Toro and I are the only friends she's got here.
column2: 「是時候該有人在太晚前幫助艾爾莎了!我和托羅是她在這兒唯一的朋友。」

column1: She's a lot like her dad, though.  She organized these brigands and changed them from mere thugs to major raiders!
column2: 「不過她很像她爸爸。她組織了這些山賊，把他們從小流氓變成大盜匪!」

column1: She plans to move the entire band away from the valley as soon as the snow clears.  If that happens, I know she will never be disenchanted.
column2: 「她計畫一旦積雪融化就帶著整個幫派離開山谷。要是那樣，我就知道她永遠不會被解除詛咒。」

column1: You know about the enchantment?  Then perhaps you have come to help.  Perhaps you are able to take the child from the band.
column2: 「你知道詛咒的事?那你可能是來幫忙的。也許你有辦法把孩子從山賊手中救出。」

column1: Elsa doesn't know who she really is.  That's part of the enchantment.
column2: 「艾爾莎不知道她真正是誰。那是詛咒的一部分。」

column1: When Elsa was left with some brigands ten years ago, all her memories were taken away from her.
column2: 「十年前艾爾莎被留給山賊時，她的所有記憶都被奪走了。」

column1: I borrowed a Magic Mirror from a friend to try to break the spell, but it only works when spells are cast directly at you.
column2: 「我從朋友那兒借了面魔鏡想破解詛咒，不過它只有在法術直接施加在你身上時才有效。」

column1: If you're going to be the big Hero, I hope you brought a Dispel Potion or something.
column2: 「如果你要做大英雄，希望你帶了解除詛咒藥劑之類的。」

column1: Alas, poor Yorick!  I know me well.
column2: 「唉呀，可憐的尤瑞克！我太瞭解我自己了。」

column1: Ten years ago, I swore I would find Elsa no matter what.  It took me two years to find her, here with the brigands, and I've posed as her warlock ever since.
column2: 「十年前，我發誓一定要找到艾爾莎，無論如何。我花了兩年時間才在這裡找到她跟這些山賊在一起，從那時起我就一直偽裝成她的巫師。」

column1: I couldn't break the spell on her, nor could I leave her to grow up into an attractive woman surrounded by rogues and scoundrels.
column2: 「我無法破除她身上的詛咒，也無法任由她長大成為一個被無賴和騙子包圍的美女。」

column1: Someone had to defend her honor!
column2: 「得要有人為她的名譽辯護！」

column1: Can't you see that I am he?  I try to inspire (before they expire) a sense of awe and majesty in those that enter here.
column2: 「你沒看出來我就是他嗎?我試圖在進入這裡的人心中喚起(在他們完蛋前)敬畏和威嚴的感受。」

column1: After all, I am the Brigand Warlock.  One has to keep up one's appearances, or appear to keep up with one's peers.
column2: 「畢竟，我是山賊巫師。一個人必須維持自己的門面，或看起來要跟上自己的同僚。」

column1: Everybody needs to know a few magic tricks to get by these days.  You'd be surprised how effective Sleeping Powder is for taking out the guards of a caravan.
column2: 「現在誰都得知道幾招魔法才能活下去。你會驚訝於催眠粉對付商隊守衛有多有效。」

column1: You snooze, you lose.  And my banana trick has appeal of its own.
column2: 「你打瞌睡就會輸。而且我的香蕉戲法有其獨特的魅力。」

column1: For thirty years I was the Court Jester.  Now I jest enjoy watching other people take the pratfalls.
column2: 「三十年來我都是宮廷弄臣。現在我只是喜歡看別人出洋相。」

column1: I'll protect the Brigand Leader with everything I've got.  That's no laughing matter as I get madder.
column2: 「我會用盡全力保護山賊首領。這不是開玩笑，當我生氣時尤其如此。」

column1: The true Dispel Potion will break an enchantment.  You must splash the potion on Elsa as soon as you can, or she'll skewer you for sure.  She's more than a match for old Swordy Lordy" now!"
column2: The true Dispel Potion will break an enchantment.  You must splash the potion on Elsa as soon as you can, or she'll skewer you for sure.  She's more than a match for old Swordy Lordy" now!"

column1: Of which potion do you speak?
column2: 「你說的是哪種藥水？」

column1: You must know the Blade Braggart".  He used to show off in the castle courtyard all the time."
column2: You must know the Blade Braggart".  He used to show off in the castle courtyard all the time."
