column2: As I entered the cave, the Kobold ordered ME to get out.

column1: I informed it that I was Baronet Barnard von Spielburg, and heir to the lands and all within.  I ordered it to bow before me.
column2: I informed it that I was Baronet Barnard von Spielburg, and heir to the lands and all within.  I ordered it to bow before me.

column1: Then it cast a spell upon me, and I remember very little after that.
column2: Then it cast a spell upon me, and I remember very little after that.

column1: I would prefer not to talk about that.
column2: 「我寧願不談這個。」

column1: As you can plainly see, Barnard is back to his old self, thanks to you.
column2: As you can plainly see, Barnard is back to his old self, thanks to you.

column1: Now that my son is back, I have much to do.  Kindly restrict your questions to matters of import.
column2: Now that my son is back, I have much to do.  Kindly restrict your questions to matters of import.

column1: That is of no importance to your quest, and time is short.
column2: That is of no importance to your quest, and time is short.

column1: A servant is awaiting you outside to show you to your quarters.  We will see you at supper later.
column2: 「有個僕人在外面等著帶你到你的房間。我們晚餐時見。」

column1: Ello.  'Ow are you?  'Ave we met before?
column2: 「嗨。你好嗎？我們見過面嗎？」

column1: I'm 'Enry the 'ermit, that's me.  Me Farther was an 'ermit and me Murther was an 'ermit sos I come by me job rightly.
column2: I'm 'Enry the 'ermit, that's me.  Me Farther was an 'ermit and me Murther was an 'ermit sos I come by me job rightly.

column1: Don't 'ave too many visitors.  'Ermits don't, you know.  (Part o' the job description.) I likes to see a new face, though.
column2: 「沒太多訪客。隱士就是不會有很多訪客，你知道。（那是職業的一部分。）但我還是喜歡看到新面孔。」

column1: Good to 'ear anurther's speaking besides meself.  Sos wot can I do for you?
column2: 「很高興除了自己還能聽到別人說話。那麼我能為你做什麼？」

column1: E's me friend, 'e and Fenrus.  Powerful wizard 'e is.
column2: 「他是我的朋友，他和芬魯斯。他是位強大的巫師。」

column1: I 'ere 'is 'ouse is sumthin' to see up north.
column2: 「我聽說他的房子在北邊，值得一看。」

column1: I don't make it out much, and 'e's pretty busy, sos I don't see 'em too often.  But when we do all get together, you can be sure we'll 'ave fun.
column2: 「我出門不多，他也很忙，所以我不常見到他們。但當我們聚在一起時，保證會很開心。」

column1: 'E loves to play 'is games, 'e do.
column2: 「他喜歡玩他的遊戲，真的。」

column1: Fenrus is Erasmus' familiar or is that the other way around?
column2: 「芬魯斯是伊拉斯謨的助手，還是說反過來啊？」

column1: Erasmus' favorite game is the Mage's Maze.  'Ave to be a magic user to play it, though.  Erasmus is awful good at it.
column2: 「伊拉斯謨最喜歡的遊戲是法師迷宮。雖然只有法師才能玩。伊拉斯謨特別擅長。」

column1: Nasty sorts, they is.  I sees them sneaking arund the woods all the times.  Uther then the Warlock, I 'as nothing to do with them
column2: Nasty sorts, they is.  I sees them sneaking arund the woods all the times.  Uther then the Warlock, I 'as nothing to do with them

column1: E's not so bad.  Got a good sense o' 'umor, 'e 'as. I get the giggles just to think of 'im.  He heh he heh he.
column2: 「他還不錯。很有幽默感。我一想到他就咯咯笑。哈哈哈哈哈。」

column1: 'E's come by at times to chat.  Borrowed the mirror wot I borrowed from Erasmus 'e did.
column2: 「他有時會來聊天。他借走了我從伊拉斯謨那兒借來的鏡子。」

column1: A magic mirror of reflection, it was.  If you use it, when a nasty spell was cast at you, it was wot sent it back at the one wot cast it.  Do un to others, I sez.  Heh, he, he.
column2: 「那是一面魔法反射鏡。要是有人對你施壞法術，它會把法術反射回施術者。就是以其人之身還治其人之身。哈，呵呵，呵。」

column1: Well, it's dark and damp and the waters roars sos there's not a moment's quiet.  The ceiling drips and the walls are slimy and it's always cold.  But, oh well, sez I, it's 'ome.
column2: Well, it's dark and damp and the waters roars sos there's not a moment's quiet.  The ceiling drips and the walls are slimy and it's always cold.  But, oh well, sez I, it's 'ome.

column1: A 'ermit is one wot lives alone, far away from them wot talks too much or asks too many questions.  'Ermits are shy, quiet types wot don't say much.  Me brother 'Arry 'ardly says a word in a year.  Me sister 'Ortense 'asn't spoken since she were six.  I never talks at all.  Yes, we 'ermits nose how to keep a mouth shut.
column2: A 'ermit is one wot lives alone, far away from them wot talks too much or asks too many questions.  'Ermits are shy, quiet types wot don't say much.  Me brother 'Arry 'ardly says a word in a year.  Me sister 'Ortense 'asn't spoken since she were six.  I never talks at all.  Yes, we 'ermits nose how to keep a mouth shut.

column1: Me family lives pretty far apart from each other. We're all 'ermits, you know.
column2: 「我們一家住得很散。我們都是隱士。」

column1: I'm 'enry.  'Enry the eighth I is.  Me farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry. And every one was an 'ermit.
column2: I'm 'enry.  'Enry the eighth I is.  Me farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry. And every one was an 'ermit.

column1: I likes to play the game.  I can show you how to play, if you likes.
column2: 「我喜歡玩遊戲。要是你有興趣，我可以教你怎麼玩。」

column1: It's the spell wot sets off uther spells.
column2: 「那是用來啟動其他法術的法術。」

column1: Like when you wants to see the ladder, you casts a trigger and there you 'ave it, large as life.
column2: 「比如說，你想看梯子時，你就施一個觸發術，嘿，梯子就在眼前。」

column1: Course, you gots to 'ave a spell on the invisible ladder to make it visible first.  But it 'as its uses.
column2: 「當然，你得先對隱形的梯子施法讓它現身。但它確實有用。」

column1: I thinks I 'ave a scroll with the spell, if you be knowing sum magic, that is.
column2: 「我想我有個捲軸裡有法術，假如你懂點魔法的話。」

column1: The spells wot I nose is pretty simple maybe, but I like 'em.
column2: 「我懂的法術或許很簡單，但我喜歡。」

column1: I can make me ladder show up and me door close and even get rid o' pests using the trigger spell, you see.
column2: 「我可以用觸發術讓梯子出現，讓門關上，甚至除掉害蟲，你看。」

column1: I nose sum magic.  Erasmus the wizard tot me sum spells.
column2: 「我懂一些魔法。法師伊拉斯謨教了我一些法術。」

column1: He's wot put the spell on me ladder sos the brigands don't get up.  Them wot climbs th' rocks gets a door opening in their nose.
column2: 「他就是在我的梯子上施法，讓山賊爬不上去。那些爬岩石的傢伙會被門打在鼻子上。」

column1: This 'ere's wot's called the Flying Falls.
column2: 「這個地方叫做飛瀑。」

column1: I've doon sum flying falls arund 'ere meself. That porch gets slippery sumtimes.  He, heh, he.
column2: 「我自己也在這附近飛過瀑布。那個陽台有時會很滑。哈，呵呵，呵。」

column1: Confidently, you cast the Calm spell.
column2: Confidently, you cast the Calm spell.

column1: Why, how cute!  You cast the Calm spell, and the monster visibly relaxed.  Why, now it's calmly and relaxedly ripping you to shreds and eating you.
column2: Why, how cute!  You cast the Calm spell, and the monster visibly relaxed.  Why, now it's calmly and relaxedly ripping you to shreds and eating you.

column1: It was a tough battle, and you lost.\nNever fear!  All you have to do is restore your game, and...\nWhat do you mean, "Restore WHAT game?"
column2: 這場戰鬥很激烈,你輸了。別害怕!你只需要讀檔,然後……等等,你說什麼「讀什麼檔?」

column1: You're hopelessly surrounded.  You should have stopped those brigands somehow.  Maybe you'll see the light.
column2: 你無可救藥地被包圍了。你應該想辦法阻止那些山賊。也許你會看到光明。

column1: You'd better find a way to prevent the brigands from getting at you from all sides if you want to survive this.
column2: 你想活下來就得想辦法防止山賊四面夾擊。

column1: Oh, a wise guy, eh.
column2: Oh, a wise guy, eh.

column1: Die drei Knochelkopfen grab you off the table.  You should have waited until they were out of reach.
column2: 三個骷髏頭怪搶過去把你從桌上抓走。你應該等到他們夠不著你再說。

column1: You're hopelessly surrounded.  You should have taken quicker action against these Knochelkopfs.  Elevate your thoughts to a higher plane.
column2: 你無可救藥地被包圍了。你應該更快對付這些骷髏頭怪。把你的思想提升到更高的境界。

column1: You move the chair in front of the door.
column2: 你移動椅子到門前。

column1: There are still too many brigands for you to fight in here.  You need to find some way to block one of the doors.
column2: 這裡的山賊還是太多,你打不過。你得想辦法封住其中一扇門。

column1: Wooden tables, benches, and chairs fill the space of this brigand meeting area.  A chandelier lights the room and an unlit candelabra stands in the corner.  A rope hangs from the chandelier.
column2: 木桌、長凳和椅子填滿了這個山賊集會區。一盞吊燈照亮房間，角落裡站著盞未點燃的燭台。繩子從吊燈垂下。

column1: There is a chandelier with a rope attached.
column2: 有一盞吊燈上面綁著繩子。

column1: The floor is strewn with flotsam and jetsam.
column2: 地板上散落著各種廢棄物。

column1: You see a door .
column2: You see a door .

column1: The door you came through is the only object you see.
column2: The door you came through is the only object you see.

column1: Most of the tables are grimy with dried beer and such.  The head table, though, is clean.
column2: 大部分的桌子都粘著乾燥的啤酒漬之類的東西。不過主桌看起來很乾淨。

column1: The chandelier is firmly attached to the rope in order to lower it to light the candles.
column2: The chandelier is firmly attached to the rope in order to lower it to light the candles.

column1: The massive candelabra certainly seems to get in the way.
column2: The massive candelabra certainly seems to get in the way.

column1: It says, "Brigands over all" (only in German).
column2: It says, "Brigands over all" (only in German).

column1: They remind you of a bunch of Knochelkopfs.
column2: 它們讓你想起一群 Knochelkopfs(碰頭怪)。

column1: The rope is heavy and very sturdy.  It is secured to the chandelier.
column2: The rope is heavy and very sturdy.  It is secured to the chandelier.

column1: You don't dare; the brigands are coming.
column2: You don't dare; the brigands are coming.

column1: The door is locked from the other side.
column2: The door is locked from the other side.

column1: You're not near a door.
column2: You're not near a door.

column1: That wasn't at all helpful.
column2: That wasn't at all helpful.

column1: There are no rocks in here.
column2: There are no rocks in here.

column1: The door is closed, but has no lock.
column2: The door is closed, but has no lock.

column1: The door is locked.
column2: 門是鎖著的。

column1: The door is already blocked.
column2: 門已經被堵住了。

column1: Get closer to the chair.
column2: Get closer to the chair.

column1: The door is closed but has no lock.
column2: The door is closed but has no lock.

column1: The candelabra won't budge.
column2: The candelabra won't budge.

column1: It's much too heavy to carry.  You can barely move it.
column2: It's much too heavy to carry.  You can barely move it.

column1: Just keep a cool head and be logical.  You don't need to run or sneak.
column2: Just keep a cool head and be logical.  You don't need to run or sneak.

column1: Welcome!  Welcome, traveler, to the Hero's Tale Inn!  I am Shameen, at your service.  May you find what you seek here.
column2: 「歡迎!歡迎,旅人,來到英雄客棧!我叫沙敏,為你效勞。希望你能在這裡找到你要找的東西。」

column1: I am sorry, but I'm much too broken to talk about such things right now.  I have lost everything!
column2: I am sorry, but I'm much too broken to talk about such things right now.  I have lost everything!

column1: The merchant is a rather fat man with a small moustache and beard.  He wears a turban.
column2: 商人是個相當肥胖的傢伙,長著小鬍子和鬍鬚。他戴著頭巾。

column1: The merchant consumes your meal in record time.
column2: The merchant consumes your meal in record time.

column1: He then procedes to finish your drink in one gulp.
column2: He then procedes to finish your drink in one gulp.

column1: The merchant gratefully accepts your food ration, and consumes it in record time.
column2: 商人感激地接受你的食物配給,並以創紀錄的速度吃完了它。

column1: You can't give what you don't have.
column2: You can't give what you don't have.

column1: The merchant finishes your drink in one gulp.
column2: The merchant finishes your drink in one gulp.

column1: It is the merchant's hat.
column2: It is the merchant's hat.

column1: It is the merchant's turban.
column2: It is the merchant's turban.

column1: There were about twelve brigands, including a Minotaur, if you can believe it!  Their leader was wearing a hooded cloak.  I could not see the face, but he had a high-pitched voice.  There was also some sort of warlock who giggled a lot.
column2: There were about twelve brigands, including a Minotaur, if you can believe it!  Their leader was wearing a hooded cloak.  I could not see the face, but he had a high-pitched voice.  There was also some sort of warlock who giggled a lot.

column1: Although worn with age, the brigand leader's cloak must have, at one time, been an exquisite garment.  No doubt it was stolen, as were all of my goods!
column2: Although worn with age, the brigand leader's cloak must have, at one time, been an exquisite garment.  No doubt it was stolen, as were all of my goods!

column1: Perhaps it was my eyes that deceived me, but I could swear there was Gnome blood in that odd magician.
column2: Perhaps it was my eyes that deceived me, but I could swear there was Gnome blood in that odd magician.

column1: The magician who travels with the brigands is no wizard.
column2: The magician who travels with the brigands is no wizard.

column1: It is my hat.
column2: It is my hat.

column1: It is my turban.
column2: It is my turban.

column1: I am Abdulla Doo, son of Ali, grandson of Hasan, and formerly Master Merchant of Shapeir.  Now I am but a penniless burden upon my friends.
column2: I am Abdulla Doo, son of Ali, grandson of Hasan, and formerly Master Merchant of Shapeir.  Now I am but a penniless burden upon my friends.

column1: Even though they, too, lost a fortune when the brigands stole from me, Shameen and Shema are caring for this frail shadow of a great man.
column2: 「儘管沙敏和席瑪在山賊從我這裡偷走東西時也失去了一筆財富，他們仍在照顧這位偉大男人的脆弱影子。」

column1: Oh, I cannot bear to talk about my family at this sad time!
column2: Oh, I cannot bear to talk about my family at this sad time!

column1: You must not have heard of the brigands who prey upon the innocent and unwary.
column2: You must not have heard of the brigands who prey upon the innocent and unwary.

column1: All that I once owned is gone, alas!
column2: All that I once owned is gone, alas!

column1: Who would have thought that there could be such trouble entering this valley during the off-season?
column2: Who would have thought that there could be such trouble entering this valley during the off-season?

column1: My good friends Shema and Shameen, owners of this inn, are the finest of all the Katta people that ever graced Shapeir!  But for their kindness, I would have starved long since.
column2: My good friends Shema and Shameen, owners of this inn, are the finest of all the Katta people that ever graced Shapeir!  But for their kindness, I would have starved long since.

column1: If you have never seen such a beast before, you may count yourself lucky.  It is like a giant of a man with a bull's head.
column2: If you have never seen such a beast before, you may count yourself lucky.  It is like a giant of a man with a bull's head.

column1: Two weeks ago, I was about to become wealthy beyond my dreams.  I, Abdulla Doo, would be the first merchant into the valley this year.  But my life was shattered by those vile brigands!
column2: Two weeks ago, I was about to become wealthy beyond my dreams.  I, Abdulla Doo, would be the first merchant into the valley this year.  But my life was shattered by those vile brigands!

column1: The band of brigands ambushed us just as we crossed the pass into the valley. They first used some magic which blinded us.  Then they overwhelmed my six guards and my assistants.
column2: The band of brigands ambushed us just as we crossed the pass into the valley. They first used some magic which blinded us.  Then they overwhelmed my six guards and my assistants.

column1: All of my trade goods were taken from me, right before my very eyes!  Why the brigand leader spared my life, I do not know.  I am now but a beggar, living off the generosity of my friends.
column2: All of my trade goods were taken from me, right before my very eyes!  Why the brigand leader spared my life, I do not know.  I am now but a beggar, living off the generosity of my friends.

column1: Ah, Shapeir!  Beautiful land of golden sands and shining sun! The heart of civilization!  Alas, she is plagued with fierce Djinni and Efreets, who seek to drive all men and Kattas from the land.
column2: Ah, Shapeir!  Beautiful land of golden sands and shining sun! The heart of civilization!  Alas, she is plagued with fierce Djinni and Efreets, who seek to drive all men and Kattas from the land.

column1: But I can speak no more of the homeland I shall never see again.  Instead, I will die in this cold, forsaken land, bereft of all I love!
column2: But I can speak no more of the homeland I shall never see again.  Instead, I will die in this cold, forsaken land, bereft of all I love!

column1: Alas, had I known magic, perhaps I could have turned the tide against those abominable brigands.  Their magic did not seem so strong, but they outnumbered my poor caravan by two to one!
column2: Alas, had I known magic, perhaps I could have turned the tide against those abominable brigands.  Their magic did not seem so strong, but they outnumbered my poor caravan by two to one!

column1: My guards are all gone, killed or run away like dogs!  I, too, would be lying dead had not the strange brigand leader spared my life, for what reason I know not!
column2: My guards are all gone, killed or run away like dogs!  I, too, would be lying dead had not the strange brigand leader spared my life, for what reason I know not!

column1: Your kindness overwhelms me.  I can tell you will someday be a great hero.
column2: 「你的善良讓我不知所措。  我能看出你將來會成為一位偉大的英雄。」

column1: Now you will have a secret.  Among the items they stole from me was a magic rug.  I alone know the words to command it.
column2: 「現在你就擁有了一個秘密。  他們從我這裡偷走的物品中，有一條魔法地毯。  只有我知道驅動它的言語。」

column1: If you help recover my treasure, I will take you, Shameen, and Shema back to our land by way of the flying carpet.  Shapeir needs powerful heros, too!
column2: If you help recover my treasure, I will take you, Shameen, and Shema back to our land by way of the flying carpet.  Shapeir needs powerful heros, too!

column1: Oh, it is indeed sad and dangerous times we live in when a man who struggles daily to keep from starving should be robbed of all his earthly possessions!
column2: 「哦，確實這些是悲傷和危險的時代，一個每天都在與挨餓作鬥爭的人被搶劫了他所有的塵世財產！」

column1: Hello again, my friend.
column2: 「又見面了，我的朋友。」

column1: May you dream of all the rewards you deserve.
column2: 「願你夢到所有你應得的獎勵。」

column1: You thank Shameen and pay him 5 silvers for the room.
column2: 你感謝沙敏,為房間付了他 5 枚銀幣。

column1: I am Shema.  Allow me to serve you, Wanderer from Afar.  Do you wish food or drink?
column2: 「我叫席瑪。讓我為你效勞,遠方的流浪者。你要食物還是飲料?」

column1: I bring you that which you ordered.  May it please and satisfy you.
column2: 「我為你帶來了你所點的東西。希望它能取悅並滿足你。」

column1: You thank Shema and pay her.
column2: You thank Shema and pay her.

column1: Not until you're through with Shema.
column2: Not until you're through with Shema.

column1: You should wait for Shema to bring you what you ordered.
column2: 你應該等席瑪把你點的東西帶過來。

column1: Eat your meal first.
column2: 先吃你的飯菜。

column1: Finish your drink.
column2: 喝完你的飲料。

column1: Beyond the bear, the cave seems to continue.
column2: Beyond the bear, the cave seems to continue.

column1: The bear is gone.  All that is left is the body of a dead man.
column2: The bear is gone.  All that is left is the body of a dead man.

column1: The bear is gone.
column2: The bear is gone.

column1: He's dead, %s.
column2: He's dead, %s.

column1: The man is gone.
column2: The man is gone.

column1: There is no magic in the cavern.
column2: There is no magic in the cavern.

column1: The body lying there looks a lot like the picture of the Baronet von Spielburg on the poster at the guild hall.  You have a funny feeling that you may have made a slight tactical error.
column2: 躺在那裡的屍體看起來很像公會大廳海報上貴族少爺馮·史畢柏格的圖片。你覺得事情不對勁，好像犯了個小戰術錯誤。

column1: You have a funny feeling that you may have made a slight tactical error.
column2: 你覺得事情不對勁，好像犯了個小戰術錯誤。

column1: You have the honor of meeting the Baronet Barnard von Spielburg.  We are pleased that you broke our enchantment.  Perhaps our father the Baron will reward you should you ever visit our castle.
column2: 「你有幸遇見巴納德·馮·史畢柏格貴族少爺。我們很高興你打破了我們的魔法。也許我們的父親男爵會在你訪問我們的城堡時獎勵你。」

column1: The arrogance of the Baronet astonishes you.  Perchance the kobold Magic User had a reason for changing him into a bear.

column1: You behold a most fascinating place.
column2: 你看到了一個最迷人的地方。

column1: The person labeled "ME" speaks:
column2: 被標籤為「我」的人說話了：

column1: Abandon mope, all ye who enter here!
column2: 「所有進入此地者，都要拋棄悶悶不樂!」

column1: Job hunting, or just sight-seeing?
column2: 「在找工作，還是只是來觀光?」

column1: I foresee a brilliant future for you in the fall.
column2: 「我預見你的輝煌未來…會在秋天到來。」

column1: If you've got something to say, then speak fast or forever hold your pieces.
column2: 「如果你想說什麼，就說快點，要不就永遠閉嘴。」

column1: State your case before I case your estate!
column2: 「在我接收你的遺產之前，先陳述你的理由!」

column1: Elsa is right next door.  I'll go set up the secret exit for your escape.
column2: 「艾爾莎就在隔壁。我去準備秘密出口好讓你逃脫。」

column1: This time the joke fell flat.  That was a truly dirty trick.  Too bad you won't have a chance to get even...or will you?
column2: This time the joke fell flat.  That was a truly dirty trick.  Too bad you won't have a chance to get even...or will you?

column1: What a miserable experience!
column2: What a miserable experience!

column1: Oops!  You thought this door led SOMEWHERE!
column2: Oops!  You thought this door led SOMEWHERE!

column1: The bright smell of fresh herbs mingles with the aroma of wood smoke as you near a hut by the side of the road.
column2: The bright smell of fresh herbs mingles with the aroma of wood smoke as you near a hut by the side of the road.

column1: It takes a lot of skill and practice to climb this tree.
column2: 爬這棵樹需要很多技能和練習。

column1: You carefully bend down and reach into the nest.
column2: 你小心地彎下身子，伸手進入巢裡。

column1: In the nest, you find a gold ring.
column2: 在巢裡，你找到了一枚金戒指。

column1: You take the ring and move carefully back along the limb.
column2: 你拿起戒指，小心地沿著樹枝往回走。

column1: Made it!  Now back down the tree.
column2: 成功了！現在下樹吧。

column1: Your bug doesn't have any hands.  This makes throwing anything out of the question.
column2: Your bug doesn't have any hands.  This makes throwing anything out of the question.

column1: Looks like all you can do is wriggle.
column2: Looks like all you can do is wriggle.

column1: Better concentrate on the game.
column2: Better concentrate on the game.

column1: Schwertfisch
column2: 寶劍魚

column1: Ach du lieber
column2: Ach du lieber

column1: Schweinhund
column2: 豬狗

column1: Antwerp
column2: 安特衛普

column1: Rheingold
column2: 萊茵金

column1: Deutschmark
column2: 德國馬克

column1: Purple Saurus
column2: 紫色索魯斯

column1: The brigands were waiting for you to come back.  They expected you to make another try for some of their treasure.
column2: 山賊在等你回來。他們以為你會再試一次搶他們的寶藏。

column1: Grumble Grumble Grumble.  Stupid guard duty, third time this week.  Why always me?  Grumble Grumble Grumble.
column2: 「碎碎念碎碎念碎碎念。該死的衛兵職責，本週第三次。為什麼總是我？碎碎念碎碎念碎碎念。」

column1: I'M BAAD!
column2: 「我是個壞傢伙！」

column1: You find a good purchase on the rocks to climb.
column2: 你在岩石上找到了一個很好的立足點來攀爬。

column1: The dirt is too loose for climbing here.
column2: 這裡的泥土太鬆散了，無法攀爬。

column1: Well I'll huff, and I'll puff, and I'll smash the gate down.
column2: Well I'll huff, and I'll puff, and I'll smash the gate down.

column1: Boy that feels good.
column2: Boy that feels good.

column1: 'DING DING DING DING....' \n"Well, maybe the brigands aren't such inhospitable creatures after all."
column2: 'DING DING DING DING....' \n"Well, maybe the brigands aren't such inhospitable creatures after all."

column1: Then again, maybe they are.
column2: 不過話說回來，說不定他們是。

column1: The brigands have an even temperament...all bad!   You seem to have a knack for doing the wrong thing.
column2: The brigands have an even temperament...all bad!   You seem to have a knack for doing the wrong thing.

column1: As you reach toward the painting...
column2: 當你伸向畫……

column1: From somewhere upstairs, you hear the Sheriff shout: "Otto!".
column2: From somewhere upstairs, you hear the Sheriff shout: "Otto!".

column1: The people who own this house must have some money. Everything looks new and there is not a speck of dust visible.
column2: 擁有這棟房子的人一定有些錢。所有東西看起來都很新，看不到一絲塵埃。

column1: The room smells vaguely of sauerkraut and bratwurst, with just a faint odor of smoke from pinewood. From somewhere in the house you can hear someone snoring.
column2: 房間裡隱隱散發著酸菜和德式香腸的味道，還有一絲松木煙火的淡淡氣味。你可以聽到房子某處傳來打鼾聲。

column1: By lifting the painting, you can see what certainly must be a safe, hidden in the wall.
column2: 藉由抬起畫，你可以看到一定是隱藏在牆裡的保險箱。

column1: You carefully lower the painting into its original position.
column2: 你小心地把畫放回原來的位置。

column1: Naughty, naughty.  The Sheriff and Otto arrive on the scene and arrest you for "blatant power-gaming".  You have to *work* at it to become a *real* hero!
column2: 壞壞。治安官和奧圖現身逮捕你「明目張膽亂遊戲」。你得真的去*努力*才能成為*真正*的英雄！

column1: Obviously, you shouldn't have done that!
column2: 顯然，你不應該這樣做！

column1: Now you've done it!  It's hard to be a hero when you're locked up for breaking and entering (or entering and breaking, as the case may be).  Be a little more intelligent (and a lot quieter) if you ever try something like this again.
column2: 現在你完蛋了!當你因為非法侵入(或侵入後破壞,不管哪種)被鎖起來時,很難成為英雄。下次要是再試什麼花樣,聰明點(也安靜點)。

column1: Gently and stealthily, you lift the lid on the little box.
column2: 你輕輕地、悄悄地打開小盒子的蓋子。

column1: As the little music box begins to play, you hear the Sheriff yell out: "Otto, stop playing with that music box and GO TO BED!"
column2: 當小音樂盒開始演奏時，你聽到治安官大聲叫道：「奧圖，停止玩那個音樂盒，去睡覺！」

column1: Boy, did YOU make a mistake!
column2: 哎呀，你可真犯了個錯誤！

column1: Otto can't find the music box, but he's too dim and sleepy to figure it out, so he heads on back to bed
column2: 奧圖找不到音樂盒，但他太遲鈍又太瞌睡，想不出來，所以他就回床上去。

column1: Otto, even in his sleepy state, winds the music box and closes the lid before he heads back to bed.
column2: 奧圖即使在瞌睡狀態下，也在回床之前把音樂盒上了發條並關上蓋子。

column1: Otto stares sleepily at the closed music box for a moment and heads back to bed.
column2: 奧圖睡眼惺忪地看著關上的音樂盒片刻，然後回床上去。

column1: That was close! The goon must've been so dumb or sleepy or both that he didn't even see you standing there.
column2: 差點就暴露了！那傢伙一定是太笨、太瞌睡，或者兩者都有，才沒看到你站在那裡。

column1: Obviously, getting in the goon's way was not one of your brighter ideas. You've had it now!
column2: 很顯然,擋在手下的路上不是你的聰慧之舉。現在你完蛋了!

column1: It's hard to be a hero when you're sitting in a jail cell.  The Sheriff apologized for your broken arm, but he did warn you (didn't he?) that Otto was only partly trained.  In the future, you'll probably be more careful when you're robbing someone.
column2: It's hard to be a hero when you're sitting in a jail cell.  The Sheriff apologized for your broken arm, but he did warn you (didn't he?) that Otto was only partly trained.  In the future, you'll probably be more careful when you're robbing someone.

column1: When it comes to breaking in, it looks like the only thing broken is your head!  When at last you come to your senses, you find yourself in a jail cell.  By the time you get out of here, you'll be too old to be a hero.
column2: 說到撬鎖,看起來只有你的腦袋被撬破了!當你終於清醒時,你發現自己在牢房裡。等你出獄,你已經老到不能當英雄了。

column1: Uh Oh! As you see a pillow flying toward you, you hear the Sheriff's wife say...
column2: 哎呀！當你看到一個枕頭飛向你時，你聽到治安官妻子說……

column1: SCREEEEEEEEEEEECH!
column2: 「啊啊啊啊啊啊啊啊啊啊啊！」

column1: You never dreamed a feather pillow could be so HARD!  Or was it the floor?  You also didn't realize how hard it is for a thief to be a Hero.  The Thief of Baghdad should have this kind of luck!
column2: You never dreamed a feather pillow could be so HARD!  Or was it the floor?  You also didn't realize how hard it is for a thief to be a Hero.  The Thief of Baghdad should have this kind of luck!

column1: It looks difficult. You look around for a good place to climb.
column2: 看起來很難。你四處尋找適合爬的地方。

column2: 這看起來像個不錯的地方。

column1: You valiantly scrabble against the cliff wall, but it looks like your climbing skill could stand some improvement.
column2: 你勇敢地在懸崖壁上亂抓，但看起來你的攀爬技巧還有待改進。

column1: Perhaps there is some other way to get to your objective.
column2: 也許還有其他方式到達你的目標。

column1: You look for an appropriate place to climb.
column2: 你尋找適合爬的地方。

column1: Ohhhhhhh!
You remain unconscious on the ground for an indeterminate amount of time. As you finally revive, you realize that you've caused yourself a fair amount of damage.
column2: 你昏迷在地上好一陣子。當你終於清醒過來時，你意識到你對自己造成了相當大的傷害。

column1: Wellll...
If you're willing to do a small little teensy favor for me, I might reconsider having you for supper.
column2: 「如果你願意幫我做個小小的小忙，我也許會重新考慮把你當晚餐吃。」

Mandrake must be pulled

Hear what I say

Bring back the root

column1: This seems like a quiet little town.  On the porch ahead of you are two people.  The standing one is large, rather ugly, and playing with a yo-yo.  The seated person smoking a pipe looks like he might be the Sheriff.
column2: 這看起來像是一個安靜的小城鎮。  你前面的門廊上有兩個人。  站著的那個又大又醜，在玩溜溜球。  坐著抽煙鬥的那個人看起來可能是治安官。

column1: Otto and the Sheriff have retired for the evening.
column2: Otto and the Sheriff have retired for the evening.

column2: The Sheriff calls out behind you:  "Just a friendly suggestion, but whatever you do, don't drink the Dragon's Breath.  Not even Otto can drink Dragon's Breath!"

column2: 窗戶從裡面的燈光發出光芒。  招牌上寫著「英雄傳奇客棧」。

column2: The gate is already closed.

column2: The town gate is standing wide open, as it does every day.

column2: The sturdy town gate is completely locked and barred, as it is every evening.

column2: Only the Sheriff can unlock the gate.

column1: Spielburg remains colorful, even at night.
column2: 即使在晚上，史畢柏格依然色彩繽紛。

column1: The Barber Shop appears to be closed. There's a note hanging from the doorknob.
column2: The Barber Shop appears to be closed. There's a note hanging from the doorknob.

column1: The man with the pipe greets you.
column2: 那個抽煙鬥的男人向你問好。

column1: Welcome to our town. You are lucky to have made it down from the mountains before the snow blocked the pass again. It's gotten pretty dangerous outside of town, I understand.
column2: 「歡迎來到我們的城鎮。  你很幸運在雪再次堵住山口之前從山上下來。  據我了解，城鎮外已經變得相當危險。」

column1: Many monsters have been trapped around here with the late snow. Between them and the brigands, we certainly could use a Hero around here.
column2: 「許多怪物隨著遲到的雪被困在這裡。  在他們和山賊之間，我們肯定需要一位英雄。」

column1: I am Sheriff Schultz Meistersson. This is Otto Von Goon, my assistant. What do you call yourself?
column2: I am Sheriff Schultz Meistersson. This is Otto Von Goon, my assistant. What do you call yourself?

column1: Unknown Hero
column2: 不知名的英雄

column1: Good luck in your quest, %s.
column2: Good luck in your quest, %s.

column1: There's more than a hint of Ogre about this strange and bulky character. He seems cheerful, though.
column2: 這個奇怪且笨重的角色身上有超過一絲食人妖的氣息。  不過他似乎很高興。

column1: Schultz appears to be an affable old coot, content to smoke his pipe. There's a hardness behind his eyes, however, that gives you reason to believe that he's no pushover.
column2: 舒爾茨看起來是個親切的老傢伙，滿足於抽煙鬥。  然而，他的眼睛後面有著堅毅，讓你有理由相信他不是好惹的。

column1: It goes up and down...up and down...up and down...up and down.....
column2: It goes up and down...up and down...up and down...up and down.....

column1: The Sheriff's pipe is a truly impressive hand-carved ivory pipe.
column2: The Sheriff's pipe is a truly impressive hand-carved ivory pipe.

column1: The Sheriff informs you that he's much too busy for idle chit-chat.
column2: The Sheriff informs you that he's much too busy for idle chit-chat.

column1: Otto only expresses himself physically.
column2: Otto only expresses himself physically.

column1: Just walk around and find out for yourself.
column2: Just walk around and find out for yourself.

column1: You'll need to talk to the Baron about that. Or go ask Wolfgang down at the Guild Hall, which is just down the street.
column2: 「你需要和男爵談論這個。  或者去公會大廳找沃夫岡，那就在街道下方。」

column1: So maybe you're going to be a hero? Well, we could surely use one around here, what with all the brigands.
column2: 「所以也許你要成為英雄？  好吧，憑藉這些山賊，我們肯定能用上一個。」

column1: There's a whole band of brigands hiding out somewhere in the mountains around us. They robbed a merchant just last week. Got a fair amount of treasure, I hear.
column2: 「有一整支山賊隊伍躲在我們周圍的某個山區。  他們上週剛搶劫了一個商人。  據我聽說，他們得到了不少寶藏。」

column1: I am Sheriff Schultz Meistersson, and this is Otto Von Goon, my assistant.
column2: I am Sheriff Schultz Meistersson, and this is Otto Von Goon, my assistant.

column1: The Sheriff replies, "Why, that's me!"
column2: The Sheriff replies, "Why, that's me!"

column1: The one who was robbed? He's staying at the Inn, I believe.
column2: 「被搶劫的那個？  我相信他待在客棧。」

column1: Go talk to the Innkeeper. He will help you there.
column2: 「去和旅館老闆談談。  他會在那裡幫助你。」

column1: You should know. You came down from there.
column2: 「你應該知道。  你是從那裡下來的。」

column1: My friend, the Sheriff says, "this world's filled with dangers. Sometimes I think it might be better to choose your danger, rather than sitting around, waiting for it, as I seem to do."
column2: My friend, the Sheriff says, "this world's filled with dangers. Sometimes I think it might be better to choose your danger, rather than sitting around, waiting for it, as I seem to do."

column1: But enough of this philosophical rambling. I am content enough to be Sheriff of Spielburg.
column2: 「但這種哲學性的漫談已經夠了。  我很滿足於成為史畢柏格的治安官。」

column1: It's simple. When somone breaks the law, and we catch them, we hold them prisoner.
column2: 「很簡單。  當有人違反法律，我們抓住他們時，我們就把他們關起來。」

column1: Obtaining treasure involves putting yourself in danger. If you're brave, foolish, or lucky, you might get some treasure.
column2: 「獲得寶藏涉及冒著生命危險。  如果你勇敢、愚蠢或幸運，你可能會得到一些寶藏。」

column1: The monsters come down from the mountains every winter, but they're usually gone by now. The Baron just doesn't have enough men to handle them.
column2: 「怪物每年冬天都從山上下來，但通常到現在已經消失了。  男爵就是沒有足夠的人手來對付它們。」

column1: Wolfgang is the one to talk to about monsters. He's had a lot of experience with them.
column2: 「沃夫岡是談論怪物的人選。  他與他們打過許多交道。」

column1: Baron Stefan keeps mostly to himself at his castle north of here. We haven't seen him here in town for years.
column2: 「史蒂芬男爵大部分時間待在他在這裡北邊的城堡裡。  我們多年沒有在城鎮裡看到他了。」

column1: Wolfgang, down at the Guild hall, is the one to talk to about monsters. He's had a lot of experience with them.
column2: 「位於公會大廳的沃夫岡是談論怪物的人選。  他與他們打過許多交道。」

column1: Otto is a big help to me, the Sheriff tells you. "He's very smart for a Goon. His prisoners only suffer a few broken bones when he grabs them."
column2: Otto is a big help to me, the Sheriff tells you. "He's very smart for a Goon. His prisoners only suffer a few broken bones when he grabs them."

column1: It took a bit of doing to teach him to bring prisoners back alive, but he hardly ever forgets, now.
column2: 「花了一些功夫教他活著帶回囚犯，但他現在幾乎不會忘記。」

column1: The Guild Hall is at the Southwest corner of town. If you're looking for work, that's a good place to go.
column2: The Guild Hall is at the Southwest corner of town. If you're looking for work, that's a good place to go.

column1: Just next to the Inn is a small Magic Shop. The owner, Zara, will let you in if you have some abilities in that area. She's a strange one, all right!
column2: 「就在客棧旁邊是一個小魔法店。  老闆莎拉會在你在那個領域有一些能力的情況下讓你進去。  她確實是個奇怪的傢伙！」

column1: The Sheriff says, "You think I'm going to tell you everything I know about everything? Go look around and find things out for yourself."
column2: The Sheriff says, "You think I'm going to tell you everything I know about everything? Go look around and find things out for yourself."

column1: However, this was not a good place to sleep.  You're stiff and sore all over and your wallet feels very light.
column2: 不過，這裡不是睡覺的好地方。你全身酸痛，錢包也變輕了不少。

column1: As safe as our streets are, you still should not sleep on them.  It is a good way to get robbed.
column2: As safe as our streets are, you still should not sleep on them.  It is a good way to get robbed.

column1: You suddenly realize that you don't have enough money to purchase what you want.
column2: You suddenly realize that you don't have enough money to purchase what you want.

column1: Blustering, you say to Shema:  "Your prices are too high for me!"
column2: Blustering, you say to Shema:  "Your prices are too high for me!"

column1: As your face turns scarlet with embarrassment, Shema smiles and says:
column2: As your face turns scarlet with embarrassment, Shema smiles and says:

column1: We are sorry not to be able to show more generosity, but we, too, are victims of hard and evil times.
column2: We are sorry not to be able to show more generosity, but we, too, are victims of hard and evil times.

column1: You must pay the innkeeper for a room.
column2: 你必須為房間付錢給客棧老闆。

column1: This looks like an alpine inn, complete with dishes on the wall and heavy beams on the ceiling.  It is pleasantly warm, and the aroma of strange spices fills the air.
column2: 這看起來像是一家山區客棧,牆上掛著盤子,天花板上有粗重的樑柱。裡面溫暖宜人,奇異香料的香氣充滿了空間。

column1: You see a small, cat-like creature known as a Katta.  You've heard that Kattas are common in the southern deserts.
column2: 你看到一隻小貓咪似的生物,叫做卡塔族。你聽說過卡塔族人在南方沙漠中很常見。

column1: You see a rather attractive female Katta wearing her native costume.
column2: 你看到一隻長得相當漂亮的母卡塔族人,穿著她的民族服裝。

column1: She's not here.
column2: She's not here.

column1: The tables look like they have been rough-cut from some of the local pines.
column2: 這些桌子看起來像是用當地松木粗糙地切割而成的。

column1: One of the tables must have been made for some tall folks, perhaps Goons, Ogres, or Centaurs.
column2: One of the tables must have been made for some tall folks, perhaps Goons, Ogres, or Centaurs.

column1: The chair is finely made, in contrast to the rough-hewn tables.
column2: The chair is finely made, in contrast to the rough-hewn tables.

column1: The door with the window in it must be the door to the kitchen.  The other door goes elsewhere in the Inn.
column2: The door with the window in it must be the door to the kitchen.  The other door goes elsewhere in the Inn.

column1: The walls have an ancient, solid look, well-worn and comfortable.
column2: The walls have an ancient, solid look, well-worn and comfortable.

column1: The heavy timbers look substantial.
column2: The heavy timbers look substantial.

column1: The oak planking is well-worn, and the carpet is clean, but becoming threadbare.
column2: The oak planking is well-worn, and the carpet is clean, but becoming threadbare.

column1: The mantle above the hearth is a solid slab of wood.  There is nothing on it.
column2: The mantle above the hearth is a solid slab of wood.  There is nothing on it.

column1: The fireplace tools are placed neatly by the hearth, along with the proprietor.
column2: The fireplace tools are placed neatly by the hearth, along with the proprietor.

column1: The beverage is more fragrant than most.

column1: Baron von Spielburg was once a brave protector of our valley.  We used to adventure together when we were younger.  No brigand or monster would dare show his face here if the Baron had not angered Baba Yaga.  Now it is said that he goes nowhere and sees no one.
column2: Baron von Spielburg was once a brave protector of our valley.  We used to adventure together when we were younger.  No brigand or monster would dare show his face here if the Baron had not angered Baba Yaga.  Now it is said that he goes nowhere and sees no one.

column1: He was a hero and worthy of the name Barnard von Spielburg.  He rode off to hunt one morning five years ago, and his horse returned with large and deep claw marks.  No sign of the Baronet's body was ever found.
column2: He was a hero and worthy of the name Barnard von Spielburg.  He rode off to hunt one morning five years ago, and his horse returned with large and deep claw marks.  No sign of the Baronet's body was ever found.

column2: Elsa was a beautiful eight-year-old child with blonde hair and sky-blue eyes.  She was the Baron's pride and joy.

column1: Ten years ago she was carried off by something which came over the wall and flew off with her.  The search for her lasted many years, but at last everyone gave up.
column2: Ten years ago she was carried off by something which came over the wall and flew off with her.  The search for her lasted many years, but at last everyone gave up.

column2: Everyone, that is, except for the Baron's Jester, Yorick.

column1: Yorick was a funny little man, but brave.  He swore he would spend his life searching for the Baron's Elsa.

column1: She is the center of our problems, I think.  Baba Yaga is a powerful Ogress.  Baron von Spielburg tried to force her to leave this valley, but she cursed him.
column2: She is the center of our problems, I think.  Baba Yaga is a powerful Ogress.  Baron von Spielburg tried to force her to leave this valley, but she cursed him.

column1: Now the Baron has lost everything but his land, and I don't know how long he will keep that.  What our valley needs is a Hero.

column2: Did I ever tell you about the days when Schultz and I rid this valley of Antwerps?  Ja, we were real adventurers then, and this was a true Guild Hall.  Now we are just old men, and this is just a place to tell old stories.

column2: 「我的鎖甲能為你提供非常好的保護。不過它很重，我得向你收 500 銀幣。」

column2: 「啊！匕首的使用是一門高超的藝術。實際上，這把武器比大多數都長，但仍然容易隱藏。只要 20 銀幣，划算得很。」

column2: Adventuring rations aren't the tastiest food in the world, but they will keep you healthy and alert as you go along.  A pack of 5 rations will cost you just 5 silvers.

column2: Oh, no, I don't carry that sort of thing here.  If you want to get drunk, try the tavern.

column1: It's a very good idea to carry an empty flask or two, in case you want to pick up a liquid or something else that needs a container.  Our flasks are a great bargain at 2 silvers each.

column2: Oh, this book?  It's about an adventurer who is trying to become a Hero.  The title is 'A Hero's Death'.

column2: Oh, you don't want those.  They're for the people who live here in town.  Adventurers just need adventuring equipment.

column1: I don't like to talk about other people behind their backs.

column2: I have pigs feet, brooms, rakes, handles, pots, teapots, eggs, linen, flour, etc.."

column2: I carry weapons, armor, daggers, rations, ale flasks, and various food stuffs.

column2: Aw, you wouldn't want to buy that.

column2: You may be in luck.  I carry a modest amount of adventurer's equipment behind the counter.  Would you like to make a purchase?

column2: Oh, good! I do try to stock some things adventurers can use.  I actually would rather be an adventurer than a shopkeeper, you see.

column2: The world-famous Troll's Sweat is always fresh.

column2: 這些匕首製作精良，顯然是為了投擲而設計。除了首領盜賊似乎無窮無盡的收藏外，還有兩套配對的匕首，柄部顏色不同，用來玩「匕首擲準遊戲」。

column1: Even thieves get thirsty.
column2: 連盜賊都會口渴。

column1: You're probably wondering if there is any honor among thieves.
column2: 你可能在想盜賊之間是否有榮譽。

column1: Stealing from a dagger-throwing thief?\nThat's a dangerous (and stupid) idea!
column2: Stealing from a dagger-throwing thief?\nThat's a dangerous (and stupid) idea!

column1: Go up to the window, please.
column2: Go up to the window, please.

column1: Don't bother.  You've already paid, stupid.
column2: 「別費事了。你早就付過了，傻瓜。」

column1: Welcome to the Thieves' Guild Union, Local 1313.
column2: 「歡迎來到盜賊公會工會，第 1313 分會。」

column1: I'm sorry, but you don't seem to have enough silver for your license.  Come back when you qualify.
column2: I'm sorry, but you don't seem to have enough silver for your license.  Come back when you qualify.

column1: Our very special items are reserved for our guild members.  No scabs allowed.
column2: 「我們最特殊的物品只保留給公會成員。不收臨時工。」

column1: There you are.
column2: 「你來了。」

column1: We only deal in cash here.
column2: We only deal in cash here.

column1: We only sell lockpicks and Thief Toolkits here.
column2: We only sell lockpicks and Thief Toolkits here.

column1: We buy only from our members.
column2: 「我們只跟成員收購。」

column1: You can't fence something you don't have.
column2: You can't fence something you don't have.

column1: We have no interest in those.
column2: We have no interest in those.

column1: Let's see now... deducting the Guild cut, and allowing for resale markup, that comes to...
column2: Let's see now... deducting the Guild cut, and allowing for resale markup, that comes to...

column1: %d silvers.  Here you are.
column2: %d silvers.  Here you are.

column1: You now have %d gold and %d silver coins.
column2: You now have %d gold and %d silver coins.

column1: A dagger is a thief's best friend, and throwing from a distance is the safest and best way to use it.  If you think you are good enough, perhaps we could play a little game.
column2: A dagger is a thief's best friend, and throwing from a distance is the safest and best way to use it.  If you think you are good enough, perhaps we could play a little game.

column1: We use the specially designed Dag-Nab-It board you can see there on the wall.
column2: We use the specially designed Dag-Nab-It board you can see there on the wall.

column1: We could start small, perhaps five silvers a game, perhaps even less.  Of course, we can wager more if you think you can take me.
column2: We could start small, perhaps five silvers a game, perhaps even less.  Of course, we can wager more if you think you can take me.

column1: There are only a few members of our Guild still around here.  With the snow and the brigands keeping away the tourists, it's hard to earn a decent living.
column2: There are only a few members of our Guild still around here.  With the snow and the brigands keeping away the tourists, it's hard to earn a decent living.

column1: Where else but here can one mingle freely with others of our chosen profession, sharing tips and secrets?  Where else can you safely fence stolen items?
column2: Where else but here can one mingle freely with others of our chosen profession, sharing tips and secrets?  Where else can you safely fence stolen items?

column1: Where else can you ask questions like these and not get thrown into jail or attacked?  The Guild is truly your home away from home.
column2: Where else can you ask questions like these and not get thrown into jail or attacked?  The Guild is truly your home away from home.

column1: Those who do not pay their union dues are the lowest form of life.
column2: 「不交工會費的傢伙是最低等的生物。」

column1: My advice is, don't get caught.
column2: My advice is, don't get caught.

column1: Now that would be telling.
column2: Now that would be telling.

column1: Just give your stolen items to Boris and you will be suitably recompensed.  Or, in terms more suited to your apparent intellect, you get cash for your loot.
column2: Just give your stolen items to Boris and you will be suitably recompensed.  Or, in terms more suited to your apparent intellect, you get cash for your loot.

column1: Boris is our accountant.
column2: 「柏瑞斯是我們的會計。」

column1: Crusher is our personnel manager.
column2: 「克拉什是我們的人事經理。」

column1: Otto is the Sheriff's goon.  He shouldn't give you any trouble unless you happen to stand in his way.
column2: Otto is the Sheriff's goon.  He shouldn't give you any trouble unless you happen to stand in his way.

column1: The Sheriff provides a useful service in this town.  He arrests anyone too stupid or unlucky to be a good thief.
column2: The Sheriff provides a useful service in this town.  He arrests anyone too stupid or unlucky to be a good thief.

column1: I am known as 'The Chief'.  But you can call me 'Chief'.
column2: I am known as 'The Chief'.  But you can call me 'Chief'.

column1: These non-union cretins are giving thieves a bad name.  I'm sure they have a spy planted in our organization, but I don't know who it is.
column2: These non-union cretins are giving thieves a bad name.  I'm sure they have a spy planted in our organization, but I don't know who it is.

column1: There is certainly a traitor in our midst.
column2: There is certainly a traitor in our midst.

column1: There are a few places around town where a clever and skilled thief can make some silver.  Perhaps even you could steal something.
column2: 「城鎮裡有幾個地方，聰明又靈巧的盜賊可以撈到銀幣。也許連你都能偷到東西。」

column1: I have had beginners before, but this Guild reached a new low when you joined.  Child, you are a thief.  Stealing is how you make a living.  So go out there and steal something and hopefully you'll get, umm, that is, you *won't* get caught.
column2: I have had beginners before, but this Guild reached a new low when you joined.  Child, you are a thief.  Stealing is how you make a living.  So go out there and steal something and hopefully you'll get, umm, that is, you *won't* get caught.

column1: We will gladly sell you a lock pick for a modest fee.  However, for the true professional, nothing will do but the Guild-Approved Acme Thief Tool Kit.  This kit will allow you to pick anything with a little practice, and is a real steal at only one hundred silvers.  Just talk to Boris.
column2: We will gladly sell you a lock pick for a modest fee.  However, for the true professional, nothing will do but the Guild-Approved Acme Thief Tool Kit.  This kit will allow you to pick anything with a little practice, and is a real steal at only one hundred silvers.  Just talk to Boris.

column1: Now that has to be one of the stupidest things anyone has ever asked me.
column2: Now that has to be one of the stupidest things anyone has ever asked me.

column1: Beginners!  Why do they keep giving me beginners?
column2: 「新手！為什麼老是給我配上新手？」

column1: I tell you, Crusher, that a thief of my stature and abilities should be stuck in a podunk town besieged by brigands who aren't even Union Members is the height of injustice!
column2: I tell you, Crusher, that a thief of my stature and abilities should be stuck in a podunk town besieged by brigands who aren't even Union Members is the height of injustice!

column1: It will cost you 25 silvers to work in town.  Just pay Boris at the door.  If you don't have the cash, go steal something.
column2: It will cost you 25 silvers to work in town.  Just pay Boris at the door.  If you don't have the cash, go steal something.

column1: A word of warning to you -- don't drink the Dragon's Breath!
column2: 「給你個忠告──別喝龍息酒！」

column1: Alms?  Alms for the poor?
column2: 「施捨？誰能施捨一點給窮人？」

column1: About time I got back to work.  Alms for the poor.  Alms!
column2: 「該回去工作了。施捨給窮人。施捨!」

column1: My name is Sam.  I've lived here for five years now, and I think I'll be heading on once the pass clears of snow.  There's supposed to be some good begging towns to the south.
column2: My name is Sam.  I've lived here for five years now, and I think I'll be heading on once the pass clears of snow.  There's supposed to be some good begging towns to the south.

column1: A beggar gets cash only when others get cash, and nobody's got much money here right now.
column2: A beggar gets cash only when others get cash, and nobody's got much money here right now.

column1: There's honest work up at the Baron's castle, I hear.  But begging's my business.
column2: 「聽說男爵城堡裡有誠實的工作。但要飯是我的行當。」

column1: I remember when people used to stroll around town at night.  Now, the only people out late are the thieves.  And outside of town, man, you don't dare go out there after dark.  The Night Gaunts'll get you for sure.
column2: I remember when people used to stroll around town at night.  Now, the only people out late are the thieves.  And outside of town, man, you don't dare go out there after dark.  The Night Gaunts'll get you for sure.

column1: The most dangerous monsters come out at night.  I'd stay inside then if I were you.
column2: The most dangerous monsters come out at night.  I'd stay inside then if I were you.

column1: Nobody's ever lived to tell what the Night Gaunts are.  I sure don't want to find out.
column2: Nobody's ever lived to tell what the Night Gaunts are.  I sure don't want to find out.

column1: Thieves prowl the streets at night.  They don't bother me because I have nothing to steal.  The brigands are worse.  They sure hurt business; no one's getting rich but them.
column2: Thieves prowl the streets at night.  They don't bother me because I have nothing to steal.  The brigands are worse.  They sure hurt business; no one's getting rich but them.

column1: You can get a job cleaning the Baron's stable, I understand.  It's good work if you want to build up your muscles or have a place to spend the night, but you won't get rich.  I'd rather beg, myself.
column2: You can get a job cleaning the Baron's stable, I understand.  It's good work if you want to build up your muscles or have a place to spend the night, but you won't get rich.  I'd rather beg, myself.

column1: Stealing from a beggar is not a heroic action.
column2: 從乞丐手裡偷東西不是英雄的舉動。

column1: Some sharp object appears to have been thrown at these walls several times.
column2: 看起來有些尖銳的物體被多次投擲到這些牆上。

column1: You can't make them out from here.
column2: 你從這裡看不清楚他們。

column1: This grubby place must be an alley.  You feel a strange, cold feeling here.  You see some discarded barrels, and a beggar holds out a bowl towards you.
column2: This grubby place must be an alley.  You feel a strange, cold feeling here.  You see some discarded barrels, and a beggar holds out a bowl towards you.

column1: A thin man with very patched clothing holds out a bowl containing only your kind contribution.
column2: 一個衣衫襤褸的瘦男人伸出碗，裡面只有你慈悲的施捨。

column1: A thin man with very patched clothing holds out an empty bowl.
column2: A thin man with very patched clothing holds out an empty bowl.

column1: The beggar's bowl contains your donation.
column2: The beggar's bowl contains your donation.

column1: The beggar's bowl looks sadly empty.
column2: The beggar's bowl looks sadly empty.

column1: Nice place.  How does this guy expect to make a living begging in this alley.
column2: Nice place.  How does this guy expect to make a living begging in this alley.

column1: It's a vessel made of staves, headings and hoops. \nMore commonly known as a barrel.
column2: It's a vessel made of staves, headings and hoops. \nMore commonly known as a barrel.

column1: This barrel reeks of Troll's Sweat.
column2: This barrel reeks of Troll's Sweat.

column1: The walls seem pretty normal, but there are some curious marks at the north ends of the buildings.
column2: The walls seem pretty normal, but there are some curious marks at the north ends of the buildings.

column1: The barrels are empty, and smell of sour beer.
column2: The barrels are empty, and smell of sour beer.

column1: Although a beggar, the man in the alley appears to be sober.
column2: Although a beggar, the man in the alley appears to be sober.

column1: The barrels are too heavy to move.
column2: The barrels are too heavy to move.

column1: You can't.  The beggar is holding onto it with an iron grip.
column2: You can't.  The beggar is holding onto it with an iron grip.

column1: Thanks.  You know, it's really tough trying to make a living begging in this town since the brigands started scaring all the trade away.
column2: 「謝謝。你知道，自從山賊把生意都嚇跑了，在這城鎮靠要飯維生就變得非常困難。」

column1: This used to be such a nice place to live.  My advice to you is not to take up begging.  It just doesn't pay.  And don't go out at night.
column2: 「這地方以前很不錯。我給你的建議是別從事要飯行業。根本賺不到錢。而且別在晚上出門。」

column1: You reach in your pocket, and suddenly realize that you have no money to give.
column2: You reach in your pocket, and suddenly realize that you have no money to give.

column1: Silver for the poor.
column2: Silver for the poor.

column1: This place seems sinister at night. You see a strangely glowing object at the far end of the alley.
column2: This place seems sinister at night. You see a strangely glowing object at the far end of the alley.

column1: These two men may be smiling at you, but they don't seem very friendly.
column2: These two men may be smiling at you, but they don't seem very friendly.

column1: The dagger looks sharp and very deadly.
column2: 匕首看起來很鋒利，非常致命。

column1: It's a glowing golden coin.
column2: 這是一枚發光的金幣。

column1: You just see a glint from here.
column2: 你從這裡只能看到一道閃光。

column1: He may be short, but the dagger makes him look 7 feet tall.
column2: He may be short, but the dagger makes him look 7 feet tall.

column1: The florid face tells you that this thief is a regular at the "Aces and Eights" tavern.
column2: The florid face tells you that this thief is a regular at the "Aces and Eights" tavern.

column1: The whole place is in need of repair.
column2: 整個地方都需要修繕。

column1: Looks like this barrel once contained Dragon's Breath.  The bottom's eaten out.
column2: 看起來這個桶子曾經盛過龍息。底部已經被腐蝕穿了。

column1: A whiff of Troll's Sweat indicates that this barrel is from the "Aces and Eights" tavern.
column2: A whiff of Troll's Sweat indicates that this barrel is from the "Aces and Eights" tavern.

column1: You're too far away from the back wall.
column2: You're too far away from the back wall.

column1: Aw, now look what ya done.  You made poor Slink run out of patience.
column2: Aw, now look what ya done.  You made poor Slink run out of patience.

column1: Aw, ya forgot to pay.  What a shame.
column2: 「啊，你忘記付錢了。太可惜了。」

column1: That's a pretty tall wall.
column2: 這是一堵相當高的牆。

column1: The thieves seem to be ignoring you.
column2: 盜賊們似乎在忽視你。

column1: Yeah, yeah, we know, you're in the Guild too.  How's a poor independent businessman supposed to make a living around here with all this competition?
column2: 「是是是，我們知道你也在公會裡。一個可憐的獨立商人怎麼樣才能在這裡的這麼多競爭中謀生呢?」

column1: Now go before I forget you made the sign and I make you pay anyway.
column2: 「現在滾蛋，趁我還沒忘記你做出那個手勢，不然我讓你付出代價。」

column1: What's that supposed to be?  You trying to pretend you're a thief or something?  Har, har.
column2: 「那是什麼東西?你在假裝你是小偷什麼的?哈，哈。」

column1: You mean to say that the first customer we've had in months is a fellow thief?  And here I thought we were going to make some money.
column2: You mean to say that the first customer we've had in months is a fellow thief?  And here I thought we were going to make some money.

column1: You had better check in at the Thieves' Guild before you get in trouble for practicing without a license.  Find Crusher in the tavern, and give him the password.
column2: You had better check in at the Thieves' Guild before you get in trouble for practicing without a license.  Find Crusher in the tavern, and give him the password.

column1: The password is '%s'.
column2: The password is '%s'.

column1: That's all right; we already have all your money.  But it was certainly nice of you to offer.
column2: That's all right; we already have all your money.  But it was certainly nice of you to offer.

column1: Hey!  You don't have enough money to be worth our while.  How do you expect us poor independent businessmen to make a living?
column2: 「嘿!你的錢不夠我們費心。你怎麼指望我們這些可憐的獨立商人謀生呢?」

column1: Slink: "Thanks, it's been a pleasure doing business with you.  You can find your way out, I'm sure.  But better hurry before my dagger starts to slip."
column2: Slink: "Thanks, it's been a pleasure doing business with you.  You can find your way out, I'm sure.  But better hurry before my dagger starts to slip."

column1: Slink:  "My finger is starting to get a bit twitchy, so forget about questions, just pay the man and get out alive."
column2: Slink:  "My finger is starting to get a bit twitchy, so forget about questions, just pay the man and get out alive."

column1: As you start to draw your weapon, the thieves jump you.
column2: 當你開始拔武器時，盜賊們撲向你。

column1: Before you can begin to use any magic, the thieves decide that you're up to no good.
column2: Before you can begin to use any magic, the thieves decide that you're up to no good.

column1: Slink says, "That coin light spell gets them every time.  See that dagger?  The next one is centered on your back.  Give Sneak there your cash and you walk out of here.  It's a simple trade, your life for your money."
column2: Slink says, "That coin light spell gets them every time.  See that dagger?  The next one is centered on your back.  Give Sneak there your cash and you walk out of here.  It's a simple trade, your life for your money."

column1: Slink:  "Hey, Sneak, this guy's hard of hearing or something.  I told him to get a license last time he was here, and he didn't listen.  Let's teach him a little lesson about listening to his betters."
column2: Slink:  "Hey, Sneak, this guy's hard of hearing or something.  I told him to get a license last time he was here, and he didn't listen.  Let's teach him a little lesson about listening to his betters."

column1: Slink:  "Why, how nice to see you again.  I do hope (for your sake) you brought us some nice shiny new silver."
column2: Slink:  "Why, how nice to see you again.  I do hope (for your sake) you brought us some nice shiny new silver."

column1: Perhaps you shouldn't explore dark alleys at night unless you are looking for trouble.  Or perhaps you should look in your manual to learn how to deal with thieves.
column2: Perhaps you shouldn't explore dark alleys at night unless you are looking for trouble.  Or perhaps you should look in your manual to learn how to deal with thieves.

column1: This tastes as sour as it smells, and it burns your throat as you swallow it. Still, it isn't the worst beer you've ever drunk.
column2: This tastes as sour as it smells, and it burns your throat as you swallow it. Still, it isn't the worst beer you've ever drunk.

column1: You know, that actually tasted fine! This really isn't such a bad place, after all, and the bartender reminds you of an old friend you used to know.
column2: You know, that actually tasted fine! This really isn't such a bad place, after all, and the bartender reminds you of an old friend you used to know.

column1: Suddenly, you don't feel so good...
column2: 突然間，你感覺不太對勁…

column1: Smells like Troll's Sweat. Tastes like Troll's Sweat. By golly, it IS Troll's Sweat....
column2: Smells like Troll's Sweat. Tastes like Troll's Sweat. By golly, it IS Troll's Sweat....

column1: You've never tasted anything like it before.
column2: 你以前沒嚐過這樣的東西。

column1: Too much beer.
column2: Too much beer.

column1: ...and one Troll's Sweat is too many.
column2: ...and one Troll's Sweat is too many.

column1: Ohhhhhhhhh woooowwwwwww!!!
Maybe you really shouldn't have tried the Dragon's Breath!  Better luck next time, and we hope you saved your game.
column2: 也許你本來不應該嘗試龍息！下次好運，我們希望你存檔了。

column1: From the size of the ancient oak kegs, you surmise that the drinking of large quantities of beverages is the main pastime in this establishment.
column2: 從這些古老橡木桶的大小來看，你推測在這間酒館裡大量飲酒是主要消遣。

column1: As dirt goes, it's pretty dirty.
column2: As dirt goes, it's pretty dirty.

column1: It's in the kegs.
column2: It's in the kegs.

column1: Mighty frothy. Looks OK.
column2: Mighty frothy. Looks OK.

column1: Looks lean, mean, and green.
column2: Looks lean, mean, and green.

column1: The fumes hurt your eyes.
column2: The fumes hurt your eyes.

column1: It doesn't look like the bartender ever wipes up spills, and the glasses are truly filthy.
column2: It doesn't look like the bartender ever wipes up spills, and the glasses are truly filthy.

column1: The man on the west side of the table is wearing a blood-stained apron, like a butcher. The man on the east has on a baker's hat. They seem to be deeply involved in their game.
column2: The man on the west side of the table is wearing a blood-stained apron, like a butcher. The man on the east has on a baker's hat. They seem to be deeply involved in their game.

column1: You can't see the game very well from where you are.
column2: You can't see the game very well from where you are.

column1: They seem to be using an "Old Maid" deck.
column2: They seem to be using an "Old Maid" deck.

column1: There are several.
column2: There are several.

column1: That's a mighty unusual keg, there.
column2: 那是個非常不尋常的酒桶呢。

column1: That looks mighty nasty!
column2: That looks mighty nasty!

column1: There are three bar stools at the bar.
column2: There are three bar stools at the bar.

column1: It looks like something you'd expect to see in a tavern, but a lot dirtier.
column2: It looks like something you'd expect to see in a tavern, but a lot dirtier.

column1: The grimy window lets little light into this tavern.  It smells like stale ale and other more unpleasant things.  The floor is covered with dirt, and the bar with sticky beer.
column2: 髒污的窗戶讓很少的光進入這間酒館。聞起來像陳舊的麥酒和其他更難聞的東西。地板覆著污垢，吧台黏著啤酒。

column1: Smoke appears to be rising from the center cask behind the bar.  To your right, two gamblers are playing cards.
column2: 煙霧似乎從吧台後面的中央酒桶升起。你右邊，兩個賭徒在玩牌。

column1: The bartender glares at you as you enter, and so does the ugly goon on the left. You get the impression that you are not welcome.
column2: 你進去時酒保瞪了你，左邊那個醜陋的打手也是。你有種不受歡迎的感覺。

column1: There seems to be something slimy spilled on top of the stool, and it is eating into the wood.  You think perhaps you should sit on the next stool over.
column2: There seems to be something slimy spilled on top of the stool, and it is eating into the wood.  You think perhaps you should sit on the next stool over.

column1: Let's finish this game first.
column2: Let's finish this game first.

column1: If you don't bet, you can't play.
column2: 「如果你不下注，就不能玩。」

column1: Now, let's not get carried away.
column2: 「現在，別太過分了。」

column1: Hey!  This is cash on the table.  You can't bet what you don't have.
column2: 「嘿!這是現金。你不能賭你沒有的東西。」

column1: So, you think you're pretty hot stuff, eh?  Well, we'll see about that.
column2: 「所以，你認為你很了不起，嗯?好吧，我們就看看吧。」

column1: You got me that time.  Say, you're pretty tough!
column2: 「你這次贏了我。說，你很強啊!」

column1: Looks like I won that one.  Must have gotten lucky.
column2: 「看起來我贏了那一局。肯定是運氣好。」

column1: It's a tie.  Let's double the wager.
column2: 「平手。我們加倍賭注吧。」

column1: Hey, nice game.  It's a tie.
column2: 「嘿，好遊戲。平手。」

column1: Shall we play again?
column2: Shall we play again?

column1: Dag-Nab-It  [score %d of 500]
column2: Dag-Nab-It  [score %d of 500]

column1: The game is called Dag-Nab-It.  You take turns with the Chief Thief throwing daggers at the board.  Each hero gets three turns, and throws three daggers in each turn.
column2: The game is called Dag-Nab-It.  You take turns with the Chief Thief throwing daggers at the board.  Each hero gets three turns, and throws three daggers in each turn.

column1: More points are scored the closer each dagger hits to the center of the board.  Highest score wins the bet.
column2: 匕首越接近棋盤中心，得分就越高。最高分贏得賭注。

column1: Adjust force and angle of throw with the arrow keys or mouse, according to your hand position.  Press Enter or click on the small dag-nab-it board to throw.

column1:
DURING THE GAME:
Click at the top of the screen or press ESC to use the menus. Additional shortcuts are shown there.
Click Right or Shift-Click Left Mouse
Button on an object to look at it.

     IN TYPING WINDOWS:
Arrows, Home and End move the cursor, or click anywhere with the mouse. Ctrl-C clears the line.

     IN DIALOG WINDOWS:
Select the outlined item with <Enter> and
use Tab and Shift-Tab to move between 
choices. 
Or click with the mouse to select an item.

ESC always cancels.
